title: Be alert at Christmas time !
review: I had the novel experience of having a financial fraud committed against me on Christmas Day ! I tried to contact Virgin Money with no success ( hence the 4 stars) , however when I did manage to speak to someone on Boxing Day he was hugely helpful and reassuring. He told me that the fraud team would be able to help me further the following day, and this they did ! I had in the mail today a letter confirming that my account has been refunded and that I would be informed of anything that they discover about the fraud.

title: Bereavement Service - A Mixed Bag
review: As the Executor on my late mother's estate, I have found the bereavement service to be a mixed bag. The initial registration process was quick and empathetic, but the process to secure funds and close the account has been anything but. This has added to the stress of dealing with the bereavement and the estate.The amount involved is also less than Virgin Money's stated threshold for requiring Probate, yet Virgin Money has required sight of grant of Probate. This doesn't stack up.

A very annoyed customer and not to mention my own waste of time . And now I'm going to be a week before we can get back for it . I fully understand I'm new to Virgin and they only have my interest at heart ..but I'm down £500 minimum as a 2nd transaction because I wasn't able to contact anyone. My confidence is rocked already as ref using the app out of hours and away from home for business purposes. Which is obviously what the card and the app is for so defeats the object slightly

title: Received a renewal quote by email for…
review: Received a renewal quote by email for travel insurance. Couldn't renew without phoning to confirm medical details. Fine, but I spent a day, on and off, phoning renewal phone numbers, general phone numbers and online chats. Eventually I got through to an agent who didn't know what he was doing. He took my medical details. Revised the quote (downwards because of reduced medical conditions) then couldn't find how to renew. Spent time consulting his manager while I was holding and came back with a quote £50 more than the renewal quote. This was attrocious service and actually quite suspicious. I closed down the call after 40 minutes wasted.If I had this amount of trouble trying to pay Virgin Money then just imagine what it would be like making a claim. I will not be using Virgin Money for travel insurance and I would certainly not recommend them.

title: Hopeless. Bye-bye Virgin Money
review: The deadline for submission of my tax return is very close and I need to get the information to my accountant within the next two or three days. Information I need includes the tax certificate for my savings accounts. For my savings with Chase, First Direct and Hargreaves Lansdowne I've been able to get this information immediately with zero delay. I tried to get this information from Virgin online but after I'd entered the required information I received a message saying I should receive my statement within the next four weeks!! Therefore I phoned their helpline. The person I spoke with said that the best she could do would be to get the information to me within one week. I informed her that unless I could get the information promptly I would be closing all of my Virgin accounts and moving to a different bank. She said that was nothing she could do about it. So bye-bye Virgin Money.

title: Useless repsonse to a fraud enquiry
review: Initially contacted by VM regarding suspected fraud on my credit card account using an SMS that looked like a phishing attempt. I ignored that and went to the app to get in touch with VM. There were 2 fraudulent transactions of over £1100 each showing on the account. Fraud support is telephone only, no email or chat.Bearing in mind that VM asked me to contact them, I expect them to answer not leave me for over 50 minutes on hold to speak to someone. No virtual queuing, no information about queue position or waiting time, just a banal recording apologising for the wait. I wanted to leave the call until they were less busy and freeze the card meantime. Card freeze function in the app wasn't working.I'll sort the fraud issues out and close the account. This isn't service. It's abuse.

By making banking better, we aim to deliver on our purpose of ‘Making you happier about money’, offering award-winning products and services to meet the full range of our 6.6m customers' retail and business banking needs. Our strategic ambition is to be the UK’s best digital bank.
title: I took out a 0% credit card and…
review: I took out a 0% credit card and transferred a balance in March 2022 with an interest free period of 28 months. In January 2023 Virgin Money staring charging interest. At no point did VM contact me to make me aware they were now charging interest. VM do not send printed statements, there is no online access and I am partially sighted so cannot use the app. I complained that they were not transparent about the charges and they never explained why they had starting charging me interest on a 0% credit card.I complained and got a standard "we are not at fault" response. To this day I still don't know why they started charging me. I cannot believe that is correct and in the interest of full transparency why was I not contacted and informed that my 0% interest rate was changing prior to the 28 month interest free period?Based on reviews it's clear Virgin Money's reputation and brand are of no value to them, which is a shame as the Virgin brand use to mean something. I have raised a complaint with the Financial Ombudsman but they are so far behind they haven't even contacted me since raising a complaint, hence I am only now posting the review on trust pilot.VM have now been acquired by Nationwide so I am hopeful that underhand practices will be a thing of the past.

title: Santander does it better.
review: I set up a balance transfer where I was supposed to have 3 months fee free balance transfers from another credit card. Alas, the app will only give me an 'exclusive offer' of 4% fee on balance transfers. I spoke with a very helpful and apologetic customer service agent who told me the fee free balance transfer can only happen through the phone. I was also told the techs are working on sorting out the issue, it's been two weeks but nothing has happened. What's the point having a part-usable app then? I am silly, I should have chosen a similar offer with Santander, their app works better and you can also use internet banking for balance transfers too. **UPDATE** I phoned Virgin Money to do a balance transfer over the telephone and after 5 minutes of getting to the correct department, the automated service told me there were not many advisers available on the phone, asked me to call at another time and hung up. I have now applied for a card with Santander.

title: Complete farce
review: I was just sat next to my wife as her anxiety levels deteriorated during what started like a comedy scetch into a farce. Having lost her credit card he wanted to request another. She was able to supply the last 4 digits she found from her bank statements. She had to repeat her address about 20 times despite giving the phonetic alphabet. They couldn't find her account, given her full name, address and last four digits. I made a suggestion, cancel your payments and see if they contact you then and find your details! She asked to speak with a manager was put on hold then the person returned and the phone hung up! I told my wife to go out and get some fresh air. :)

title: An update on my ongoing Saga with…
review: An update on my ongoing Saga with Virgin Money UK. A month later, Virgin are still refusing to send me my credit card. In fact, the fraud department refuses to even communicate with me. I have expressed that I just wish to pay off my credit card so I can get away from this awful company. Their refusal to communicate with me or accept payment suggests that they are trying to lock me into paying their fees indefinitely, as they are making it  impossible to move away from them. I have over 100 hours of calls documented over the last 2 months. I am now in a position where I have no option but to proceed with legal action for the stress they have put me through.

Welcome to Chase: the award-winning bank designed to help you make the most of your money. We're a digital bank that's part of the JPMorganChase family – and we're proud to support millions of customers in the UK.
We're here to help all our customers reach their goals, both big and small. That's why our app's packed with useful tools, insights, resources and support – so you can manage your finances all in one place.
When you spend, save and invest through the Chase app, it's all in one place – and intuitive and easy to use¹. And we have layers of security and fraud monitoring to help you keep your money, account, and personal information secure.
Ready to see what your money can do? 
Open a free Chase account today.

title: I’ve been with Chase for three months…
review: I’ve been with Chase for three months now. I wanted to set up an easy to use internet account for everyday spending. After doing some research I picked Chase for this purpose. I’m really happy to have done so as the account offers great perks such as 1% cash back on everyday spending and saving via round ups. The app is easy to use and all transactions appear immediately. Having tried lots of different accounts, including internet only accounts, Chase is definitely my favourite.

We're here to help all our customers reach their goals, both big and small. That's why our app's packed with useful tools, insights, resources and support – so you can manage your finances all in one place.
When you spend, save and invest through the Chase app, it's all in one place – and intuitive and easy to use¹. And we have layers of security and fraud monitoring to help you keep your money, account, and personal information secure.
Ready to see what your money can do? 
Open a free Chase account today.

title: Chase banking has helped me save.
review: i am into my second year using chase. The cashback on most purchases is a benefit and goes into an account with interest added monthly. The mobile app is great and there is a small amount of interest added to the mobile account each month.I like the roundup account which can help you save residual amounts of money which grows surprisingly and gains monthly interest.The second year has necessitated depositing £1500 per month to gain cashback on purchases.Extremely easy to transfer money using the app.Unfortunately, owing to purchases being made on a debit card, there is no safety net as there would be if using a credit card.Speedy response from the helpful support team if needed.Overall, I would recommend this bank.

title: Excellent bank/app
review: Excellent bank/app. Can work out cheaper than Revolut for overseas transactions, particularly with cashback.Introductory rate of cashback is excellent. I have earned over £80 in cashback since opening my account in May.I would give 5 stars were it not for the frustrations with understanding the current balance:1. I find the presentation of the current balance in the app frustrating. It is hard/impossible to know if this has been updated to the latest current balance.It would be very helpful if the balance changed colour, for example 'green' - balance has been updated or 'amber' - balance is pending (e.g. waiting for wi-fi or mobile data to update the balance).This has frequently caught me out when paying for goods or at petrol stations where a temporary debit is made at selve-serve before dispensing fuel.2. It would be useful to have text alerts, particularly when my balance falls below a set amount. This would alert me to top-up my balance before attempting a transaction

title: Not as great as I first thought..
review: Ive been with Chase since they first opened their digital bank in the UK. Brilliant experience at the start 1% cashback, 1% on current account balances and very close the BOE interest rate for demand savings.. roll on a couple of years, 1% cashback up to £15 a month, no interest on current account and now a hefty 1.5% spread on demand savings from February.. Sadly it might be time for me to shop around!

title: Been with Chase now for almost a year
review: Been with Chase now for almost a year. It's a good bank for day to day transactions but it is a nightmare if you want to move money to investment accounts. Once a month i have to call up to unblock transfers to a number of savings and investment accounts because they always flag it as possible fraud, even if you have sent money to those accounts many times before. It is a slow laborious process of calling in, justifying what the payment is for, answering loads of questions and almost having to beg to have your funds transferred to an account you own. I understand the need but when it is to the same account used all the time it can be really annoying.

title: Requesting TOO MUCH personal data
review: In the last few months, Chase have started insisting on a raft of personal information, with the threat of reducing making my access on the APP. Now, I have used Chase for quite a few years, and was happy with the APP, the customers service and the savings rates. However, despite being significantly in credit, some months back they began insisting on my supplying a raft of personal AND business data (this is a personal account, not a business account) - far beyond what any other bank I have used has requested (I have opened accounts with a good selection). My concerns are for my personal data protection, re security and 'misuse'. UPDATE: Re Chase reply to speak to their team: I have repeatedly spoke to their team, whose response amounts to  'The computer says no'.

title: Income irrelevant when not borrowing
review: The app works ok. The interest rate on savings is ok. However I removed all of  my savings once they threatened to reduce my capability, within the app, to my money. The reason for the threat was because I refused to provide an income level. They don't need this information to operate a debit card or savings account. The reason they want it is to better feed me suggestions for products. I don't want more products. I could have lied on the income level but I wasn't comfortable doing that. Therefore, to maintain my full access to my funds, I withdrew all bar pennies! ...shame really.In reply to Chase response to my initial feedback above:-They didn't deny my claim. They do not need your income but if you don't provide it then they will restrict the use of their app. ...Once again, all they want to do is send you more products ideas. I presume that I could lie (whichever way I prefer) but I'm not comfortable to do that. FYI I have had discussions with Chase on this and I'm afraid that, ultimately, "computer says no!".

title: Don’t bother if you want a dynamic and…
review: Don’t bother if you want a dynamic and customer facing bank - making a transfer for the first time can be a nightmare, even worse if they ask you to call customer services. They simply read scripts, several times over, even when you provide all the information they ask for. They hide behind the excuse of safeguarding your money, but really it’s about protecting themselves from being sued. A complaint was not upheld, even though the agent admitted that mine was by no means the only one relating to their refusal, basically, to allow me to access my own money and send some to someone I trust. Perhaps they should start listening to the customer?

What a pity.

title: A wild goose Chase
review: Awful service and incompetent ant support. I have used this bank for some time now and have current and savings accounts. Yesterday a message appeared inviting me to check to see if I was eligible for a credit card. I wanted to see what was on offer so I went through the process.After entering all the requested details I received a message saying something about a notice of correction on my credit record. I was not aware of any such notice so I went into the help messaging to ask which reference agency they had used. TransUnion apparently.  I have access to my TransUnion details via CreditKarma and MoneySavingsExpert. I checked both and there are no notices of correction.The support agent was a complete waste of time and space. He kept telling me to check TransUnion - so I send him a screen shot of the bit that said "no notices of correction recorded".  The agent wasn't interested in that.He kept telling me to follow a link in my profile to check via Chase. There is no such link.  In all it took over an hour to get nowhere with absolutely no recognition of the fact that incorrect information was being given.The basic banking service functions. Support does not.

title: I just feel I cannot trust this bank with my money.
review: The app is ok, the interest is good but I was locked out of the account and their support just couldn't care less.  Then I thought I had it sorted out (by myself) but they wanted repeatedly to identify myself ( and this is not an easy process)....  Then I was logging in when a new questionnaire came and you couldn't get past it without answering it.  They want to know how much do you earn, how much do you spend, etc., etc.  As though I was looking for a mortgage!  I moved my money and will close my accounts.  Even the higher interest is not worth the aggravation.  I just don't feel I can trust this bank with my money.

title: Chase - getting worse over time.
review: Chase has got worse over time. They capped cash-back at £15 a month, stopped allowing legitimate and legal payments to crypto exchanges and now have dropped their interest rates. The chat/customer care is still good but no doubt they'll cut that too to improve profits. A pity, they we superb to start. No different to Starling /Monzo, victims of their own success,  but soon you may as well have a Natwest account!

title: Chase are the worst bank in the world
review: Chase are the worst bank in the world, ever. They suspended my account without any sort of warning, literally was buying food and my card was declined. Phoned them up to speak to them and they asked me security questions like:What is your current account balance? I tell them a rough amount, because I don't have any access to the account I cannot tell them the exact amount. They then tell me I have failed security and won't talk to me.This has been reported to the FCA because I don't think it is legal to do this. When I have had such issues in the past the bank will ask different questions. Ones that do no require access to the account to know. I think this is just one big scam to be honest and I hope they get punished and fined for it by the regulators.

title: For no apparent reason that I can…
review: For no apparent reason that I can ascertain after a year of faultless banking my account has been withdrawn with two months notice (so clearly not a security or fraud issue) and despite promising to look into it and get back to me I have not had the decency of a reply. I have asked for documentation and other things to be supplied and I get the same reply “the team will get back to you”. Disgraceful service and I will pursue them for costs of changing direct debits etc. I had previously recommended a number of people to take an account but have asked them all to close and they are. How can a bank treat someone like this? No clue whatsoever as to what I may or may not have done or what criteria I no longer meet. I’m not the only be apparently… shame on you Chase with your trusted 50m users (allegedly)

title: Over vigilant process driven fraud team lacking in common sense and understanding. Block payments unnecessarily making the bank very difficult to use
review: I am trying to purchase a car from Tesla who bank with Citibank. Tesla did make me aware that Citibank cannot be verified through the UK banking system. The payment was flagged to the fraud team who understandably wanted more information. Whilst this is a good service provided by Chase bank to protect the interests of customers, the fraud team lack any common sense or understanding. They are a process driven team who hide behind a plethora of red flags and the FCA. Despite the fact I was able to verify the purchase was a car with a valid invoice from Tesla the fraud team would not let the payment go through . I am unable to buy my car and I have to wait up to 15 days for my payment to be investigated . Extremely disappointing.Chase need to up skill their fraud team to be able to read a situation and apply common sense rather than just following a process.

title: Experience so far is good for me:
review: Experience so far is good for me:Positives: ability to see pending directs, instant notifications, ability to search and see all transactions from a merchant and how many transactions in total, overdraft rates are tied to your credit score unlike big banks that do a blanket APR, fast and fluid app which I feel monzo is always trying to optimise, merchant logos are up to date, I can see what's left to spend after bills are taken into consideration, adding cash from other banks is a plus.  All my spending and direct debits are from monzo Worries : I have not moved my wages yet as I do a couple of transfers to house mates due to how bills and spending are done and it goes against monzo because accounts do get blocked for these reasons. Monzo support is not on the same standard as the app. Brilliant app poor customer support which feels cheaply done. I will get faster and more quality support from a big bank. Monzo needs to resolve issue very quickly especially when people don't keep cash these days.My savings and wages will move to monzo when I feel these points are addressed

title: Great but not perfect!
review: I love my Monzo account and would recommend them to anyone! But at the same time, they aren't perfect with room for improvement.The pot system they have is outstanding for managing money and completely changed how I manage my money for the better.They do have some flaws mind, they only seem to use one credit reference agency for their checks which means my mortgage doesn't show up with them.I've had a loan from them in the past paid it off early but despite being in better financial shape than before having that loan I'm no longer eligible.You are charged if you wish to deposit cash into your account.You have a limit of £1000 too and there is no workaround if you hit it.The customer service is generally ok, but at the same time could be better. For example I sent money to the wrong account once to pay a bill, but Monzo would not attempt to recover the money because my reason for making the error wasn't good enough. A mistake is a mistake regardless of the reason I give and you should have helped your customer!

title: Misleading "Get Paid Early" Feature - They Hold Your Money!
review: I’ve been using Monzo for a while, but I recently discovered that their "Get Paid Early" feature isn’t the benefit it claims to be. Payments that are supposed to arrive on Monday (e.g., student finance or salary) are held by Monzo until the last possible release date, and then they release the funds at 4 PM on the previous working day (Friday in this case).What’s frustrating is that other banks release the same payments much earlier—on Friday morning—without claiming it’s a "special feature." Monzo is effectively holding onto my money longer than necessary, likely for their own financial benefit, while making it look like they’re doing me a favor.If you’re considering Monzo for this so-called "benefit," think twice. Other banks are faster and more transparent. I’m seriously questioning their ethics and considering switching to a bank that doesn’t play these games with my money.

title: Better app than most, but bankers are bankers.
review: Better app than most, although savings are a bit hidden so not the world's best UX. But just like any other bank, they'll drop your interest rate asap without changing the fee you pay for the account. No fees on cash abroad is useful, although cash is dying in most of the EU so that's becoming less important for me personally.

title: Let down by poor customer service
review: The app is nice to use but the customer service is one of the worst I've ever experienced.  You get passed around from agent to agent, with names like Pixie and Sunshine, because they don't seem to know how to deal with issues, and they often give you standard responses without reading your message.  It seems impossible to raise a transaction dispute, which I've been trying to do for several weeks.  Mine was rejected because of no supporting evidence, but their form does not allow you to attach files.  So I was told I could attach the files to the chat, but they get lost and ignored and every time I raise the issue I go back to the beginning and go round in circles.

title: Great interface, lack of communication and consideration.
review: I started banking with Monzo in March of 2024. I genuinely loved the app, the experience, and it really helped me manage my finances and realise alot of my spending issues etc.Today 01.01.2025 I woke up to see my account had been closed. I attempted to contact monzo from my number, and it had been blocked. I called from my partners phone and they put a referral for someone to contact me within 48hrs. I assume either the issue is due to inactivity over Xmas although I see that as unlikely, maybe my recent payment to UoW for my masters degree enrollment, or potentially a query regarding my financial situation which I would be more than happy to discuss with them.I was under the impression that the instant closures were for more serious concerns, and this has caused me alot of anxiety. My financial situation is a little complex as my income is different due to tumor/health concerns which often cause me issues.I have not been given any sort of explaination or aid with resolving this. It is just done now? As a mother losing access to my bank account so suddenly just after the festive period is not acceptable really.Although their system is great, aesthetics are great too and I genuinely enjoyed the experience, I personally would not recommend banking with them to anyone following this incident.

title: Simplicity of use in the place of customer care
review: Although the app is simple and the use of the cards etc… is straightforward, the lack of customer support when some small (or large) issue arises is so problematic that you will wish you were with a high street bank. I have had a couple of small issues with Monzo over the past three years of use and they have grown into such huge problems due to a lack of communication  that I have decided to switch to a high street bank. However, now even closing an account is proving impossible as Monzo have rejected the switch and for 2 days I have been trying to rectify this issue over the phone, via the app and by email only to be told that this matter needed to be sent to a specialist team… only to be met with a wall of silence. I cannot wait to get away from Monzo however if you never have any issues regarding payments or other transactions your experience with Monzo will probably be fine. Just hope you never do.

title: Monzo Closed Account For Unknown Reason 😱😬
review: Monzo closed my account!So one day, I woke up and as usual I checked my email and shocked to see an email from Monzo saying my account has been closed. The reason is unknown.😱So, I immediately tried to open the app and it was really not logging me in. This has never happened to me before. I opened Monzo about 6 months ago. So I reached Monzo support asking the obvious question (what is the reason my account has been closed for?). Guess what they replied with……“It is confidential and we do not disclose this with our customers” what an ironic answer. To this day, I couldn’t know the reason (Monzo if you’re reading this, please at least tell me the reason). Although I have other bank accounts and nothing happened yet with them.😭Oh I really forgot to tell, I reapplied once again after 1.5 years later and guess what….. they rejected my application without any reasons except the previous closure where the reason is unknown.😭At least they paid my left over money back. Anyways, life goes on so I think I should move on from this matter. Although, it’s been over 3 years now.😀If you’re still here, stay safe, stay happy and peace out ✌️: for Monzo;(good business time Monzo)🤝

title: Ok app and bank until you need help.
review: Ok app and bank until something unusual occurs and you need their help. Customer service is poor. I raised a merchant dispute which they failed to understand and ultimately rejected months later. Basically I paid for the same thing twice due to a failure of the POS device but they never seemed to understand that. A small amount to dispute and ultimately not worth the effort. I’ll continue to use Monzo for ‘pocket money’ but keep my ‘established’ bank account for the important stuff like income and direct debits. I’m not convinced they have my back.

review: I was relocated to the UK after 1st November 2024. Starting from that date the Home Office doesn't issue BRP documents. Now it's e-visa. I used my VALID russian passport for the application and linked it with the e-visa. Was trying to create a bank account in Monzo application. It requires proof of identity. The application doesn't take e-visa share codes that contain valid information issued by the official government institute. The application takes only passport/UK driving license or BRP. I have only the passport at the moment, and so tried several times to prove my identity using it. The application keeps saying that this document is not acceptable. Tried to solve it via the customer support, but they just gave me standard response that I need to provide a valid ID.Something tells me that if it was a passport of some other "democratic" country it would work perfectly.

description:
We're building the bank of the future - complete with instant notifications, built-in budgeting tools, free payments abroad, 24/7 customer service and much more! 

Monzo is a bank for everyone, built together with our community. We're focused on solving problems, rather than selling financial products. We want to make the world a better place and change people's lives through Monzo and we think the best way to do that is by being as open as possible.

Join the revolution today.
title: Customer service is AWFUL!
review: Customer service is AWFUL! Within 2 weeks of switching to Monzo from Santander we had three fraudulent payments paid out from our Monzo account. I tried to contact someone to discuss the issue and it was an absolute nightmare. I was left on chat for almost 20 mins with no response, no idea if anyone was still there. Then, when I called to speak to someone I explained the situation, which took about ten minutes, then got told I hadn’t passed a security question so I’d have to call back and go through the whole thing again. Called back and after another ten minutes of explaining, they hung up. Called back again and exactly the same thing, I explained the situation, they hung up on me. DO NOT USE THIS BANK. I REGRET SWITCHING TO THEM AND WOULD NEVER HAVE DONE SO IF I HAD KNOWN HOW TERRIBLE THEIR CUSTOMER SERVICE WAS

title: I've been using Zopa for saving and…
review: I've been using Zopa for saving and loans, and overall, I'm quite pleased with the experience. Setting up accounts and transferring funds through their app is straightforward and efficient. Transfers between my accounts are almost instant, which is really convenient. When I did have a query, customer service initially connected me to South Africa but swiftly resolved my issue through their London team. I appreciate their competitive rates and the transparency in their loan instructions. The application process is hassle-free, and it's easy to overpay if you choose. Although I haven't needed much customer service, Zopa stands out as a reliable choice compared to others. 4 stars!

title: Cancellation is not easy
review: I am disappointed with my recent dealings with Zopa. I wanted to cancel my credit card with them, which should have been easy. No such thing. I was in credit. Was on the chat for a very long time. They said I was in debit. So I sent the same amount again to be in credit. That arrived, but Zopa said they couldn’t see it so to come back in a couple of hours!! It was on my Zopa app as a credit. I could see it. So now I have both credit amounts on my credit card so can’t close it till my refund is processed!  I have a loan account with Zopa so asked for the credit card overpayment to be credited to my loan account. They can’t do this which surprised me in this day and age. All in all I found the inordinate amount of time I spent communicating with Zopa over a simple issue to be unbelievable. In the end I left the chat and finished by speaking with the customer service team on the phone and then being transferred to a member of the “specialist team” to get the details for the refund to be issued. This may take some time because of the time of the year. Only when the refund has been processed will the credit card account be closed.

title: Website is easy to use but the loans…

title: I feel a bit "meh" about Zopa
review: I feel a bit "meh" about Zopa. I've had a loan and savings account with them for years and have just paid the loan off. But never felt much love from them. Never missed a payment and yet even now I'm apparently not eligible for another loan or credit card with them. Not that I particularly want one right now, but I can't understand why I wouldn't be eligible, having been a good, reliable customer. Now that my loan is paid off, I'll probably move my savings elsewhere as well.

Got a message saying let you know in 2 days, 3 weeks later the application disappeared, had to use the in app live chat, after a couple  of hours answering security questions gave up. It's a little more expensive but got the loan off the bank next day. Won't use them again, childish maybe but they should have treat me professionally.

title: The company is a disaster to pay early
review: Tye financing and rates process is decent. But, extra repayments are a nightmare as the company is a disaster to pay things off early with. It will take approximately 4 weeks start to finish to settle a loan early as they seem to 'hold' your payments for no discernible reason so you eventually have overdue payments. Not sure why they do this although I suspect it is to allow more interest to accrue and ultimately make you pay more. Overall, my advice would be to avoid if you can and stay with reputable banks for financing.

title: Great little banking app but could do better.
review: Great little banking app.  I can move money about quickly and easily.  Zopa annoys me by offering me a credit card firstly with a 4k limit then taking away the offer simply because I changed my address.  My financial circumstances remained constant.  They offered me a credit card again a month later with a reduced limit then withdrew the offer 3 days later. My credit score is around 850 / 999. Fed up with waiting so took out a John Lewis credit card  -  simple , straightforward application and acceptance.  I've also removed 5k of savings with zopa and placed into a Barclays rainy day savings account with instant access and 5% interest  . Zopa, you are handy but could do better.

After holding on for some time, I was advised the line I needed to be transferred to was busy so agreed a call back. It is now Tuesday, 31st December and still waiting for the call. I am surprised at the level of customer service from a Company who is holding a large amount of my funds.

title: AVOID AVOID
review: AVOID AVOIDAVOID!!!!!!! We have a car finance with this company, our car got stolen, insurance paid out on 24th dec via bacs, according to the official bacs website if paid on that date would be cleared on 30th december (today) as usual zopa have no payment their end, absolute BS!!! I have contacted them everyday asking if payment has been issued each timeIm met with ‘nothing showing our end’ they have said it will most likely be friday, FRIDAY!!!! 8 days for a bacs payment is a joke!!! They have the cash alright, just dragging their feet at settling the agreement as its accumilating interest daily while we wait! When friday comes if iGet the same crap that they have no payment ill be contacting the ombudsman! And trading standards, i have a disabled child and we cant get issued another finance untill this ones cleared, so zopa dragging their feet for their own financial gain is putting my daughters health in jeopardy as she heavily relies on a car! Stay away from this company!! They do not like you paying things offEarly!!!  Update, tesco have correct reference number and proof of cash transfer considering your a banking business, 1 you dont accept cheques which is odd, 2 iv never known a company to take so long  to receive a payment, tell you what when my next payments due that yes your still taking for a car i dont even have, ill be sure to cancel it for 8 working daya then transfer it to yourselves, lets see how long before your calling us relentlessly for payment and how quick to mark our credit !!! Jokers

title: INCOMPETENT STAFF!!!
review: Wouldn't recommend at all, had a credit card with them so thought why not try and get car finance as well. After applying I was told I was declined, then a day later got an email saying I had been accepted and money had been paid to the dealership. After trying to cancel it (within the 14 days) nothing was done so they tried to chase me for money I didn't owe as I didn't have a product to pay for. After a few months and an ombudsman getting involved they've finally accepted they have incompetent staff and removed the loan and are in the process of restoring my credit file which was heavily impacted. The only reason they're getting 2 stars is because they're removing all evidence from my credit

title: Terrible customer service
review: I had a 95 day savings account with Zopa. Never had any problems until I closed it.I tried to log on to get an interest statement.It said wrong email or password. Tried to reset password, didn't work. Got on to chat. Chat bot a waste of time. Then got a customer service agent. After wasting about 15 minutes she passed me on to somebody else. After another 15 minutes she said I had failed a security question. It was like I was trying to steal the crown jewels. I only wanted a statement.Then I was told to email their security department with a copy of my passport.I will certainly not use Zopa again.

title: Quick to start up and app is OK to use
review: Quick to start up and app is OK to use. However if you have problems with a pin number don't bother calling Zopa as you have to rest your card over 24 hours and can only get a new pin through some cashpoint machines. If your card wont work and you're in a petrol station trying to pay they won't help or authorise your card so you always need an alternative method of payment.

title: Difficultly to repay the loan
review: The loan application was easy and the money in my account no problem. However I requested an early settlement figure, got a quote and repaid my loan early (or so i thought). The money left my bank account but the loan remained open. They then logged a missed payment on my account because I had cancelled my direct debit as I had repaid the loan. I contacted them via the app and was told the money was in a holding account and my loan would be repaid 10 working days after the money left my account, WHY? 12 working days later the loan has still not been settled and they admit the money is still in my holding account. You get lots apologies but no one actually seems to be able to help and why does everything have to be done via chat!

title: unable resolve closure of account following death
review: I invested the proceeds of my mother's house sale with Zopa in a 1 year fixed savings account, acting as her attorney. She died in September, but Zopa are refusing to close the account early, preventing the execution of her will. I have spent hours waiting for resolution via chats that go dead when transferred to their specialist team. The staff are sympathetic but still no resolution.

title: This company hides behind clever small…
review: This company hides behind clever small print. Your yearly set rate of interest is heavily loaded to the start of your loan so they get a higher amount of interest at the start resulting in you losing out if you pay off your loan early. Try speaking to someone and you get bounced from one person to another with the promise of a call back and nobody calls, that’s happened twice in the last 4 days to me not a good company to deal with

title: The money came quickly
review: The money came quickly. This is about the only good experience with these guys. I have had a lot of loans in the last from lots of different company’s. I have to be honest and say that Zopa may be the worst experience I’ve ever had with any loan company. The user experience of trying to pay the loan back early is actually incredible stressful. To the extent that a £5000 extra payment had basically gone missing for nearly three weeks!! Every time I got in contact with somebody to chase it, the could see it in their ‘holding account’ but nobody could pay it to my account for varies different excuses. To be honest I think it was just a tactic to charge me more interest…After the direct debit was taken out (two weeks after the large payment), my extra payment went through (after another week) and some how, said by magic, is was the exact amount to clear the loan… to the penny!! And suddenly that was it. I find it hugely suspicious that it just so happened to be correct to the penny, I think the money that I had overpaid was just swallowed and that’s that. Really would not recommend to be honest, two stars because the money was sent to me quickly and easily, downhill from there on.

title: Do not use if you have disabilities or long term illnesses
review: Contacted to discuss our loan following a change in circumstances and severe decline in my health. Have spoken to other financial institutions who are happy to work with you. Zopa has two options, both of which weren't flexible or accommodating. The first person I spoke to pretty much said that, even if you have a payment plan, they will still harass you with collection calls which I think is appalling considering this could cause my health to relapse from the stress. The second lady was absolutely lovely but couldn't do anything either. Essentially, they don't care if their actions will lead you to be hospitalised. We're not even in arrears and I was trying to be proactive to make changes moving forwards. I wanted to raise a complaint but this didn't happen and there's no way of doing this without going through phone calls or webchat (webchat was unresponsive for about 10 minutes, even though I was connected to an agent, before I gave up. Phone call lasted almost an hour and didn't get anywhere). Will never use them again

title: Absolutely terrible dealing with me
review: Absolutely terrible dealing with me. I informed them back in November 2024.I have sent proof from doctors letters / Consultant letters even a photo of the meds I'm on for terminal prostate cancer.All my other credit cards have issued medical write off. But this company just keeps on adding interest monthly. Take no notice of my condition.I have sent over 27 emails to them & just been fobbed off. No specialist Manager like all other credit card company. This company does not know how hard it is to go through Radiotherapy & Chemotherapy.  Stay well away from this uncaring company.Even PAYPLAN who they tell you to contact. Told me to go directly to the Financial Ombudsman & the FCA. So that is what is happing.

Highly unprofessional, I would certainly warn anyone against using Zopa for the foreseeable future.

title: Zero Outstanding Practical Assistance
review: Zopa: The Comedy of Errors in Modern Banking”Oh, Zopa. Where to begin with such a stellar display of inconsistency and head-scratching logic? Let’s kick off with their credit card offering. They handed me a massive £300 credit limit—truly life-changing, right? For 18 months, I used this card responsibly, all while reducing my overall debt from £20K to £7K. Yet, despite proving I could manage my finances like a pro, Zopa’s system of “pathetic point-scoring” decided I wasn’t worthy of a single limit increase.Now, here’s the punchline: while refusing to trust me with a higher credit limit, they were more than happy to throw me a £5K loan. Yes, the same Zopa that thought £300 was my ceiling suddenly believed I could handle a multi-thousand-pound commitment. Comedy gold, isn’t it? Naturally, I closed the card myself, because why bother with a company that can’t even follow its own logic?And the loan? Let’s just say Zopa’s “customer service” proved itself to be as helpful as a chocolate teapot. Communication breakdowns and general incompetence seem to be their specialty. It took significant effort and outside intervention to finally resolve the mess they created.Zopa’s motto must be, “We can give you a loan, but we can’t give you sense.” Never again. Zopa, you’ve earned your place in the Hall of Fame—for all the wrong reasons.

title: Hopeless
review: Currently have two cash ISA accounts with them. I just wanted to check how much the interest rate would be if I transferred another ISA into them. All I could find online was a particular figure which didn’t specifically relate to transfer in. Also, it said for new cash ISAS and did not mention transfer in. I started off by using the online chat and after getting through to a human was involved in a two and fro conversation with her who asked me lots of information and all I wanted was a simple answer. She then disappeared off the chat and didn’t come back. I then decided to phone the company up. I spoke with someone who said it’s a variable rate and therefore they can’t tell me what the rate is. I said I just need to know what today’s variable cash ISA rate is on a cash ISA transfer. He said that was not possible to give me that information. I asked to speak to a manager and was kept waiting. He then came back to me and said there’s no manager available as they are all in meetings. Can I transfer you to another advisor?  I said okay and then the waiting began again for another further 30 minutes. I just put the phone down after that. So all in all as an existing customer I was on the phone for an hour and 30 minutes to find the answer to the question of what is your interest rate for transfer in cash ISAS today? I appreciate the caveat is that the rate may be different on actual transfer. That question could not be answered. I have since decided to move my transfer I had phoned about somewhere else and I will be moving my existing ones as well very soon..

title: The application process is great but...

I made an overpayment to my account 2 weeks ago and despite numerous contacts with the online chat telling me it would be resolved it still hasn't. It just seems to have disappeared into some holding account ether. I now have a regular payment due on my loan that should have been significantly reduced but now won't be. There's no reason why in 2024 the process should be this difficult.

title: Frustrating customer experience
review: I've had an account with Zopa for years. You have to link a bank account and I now have 5 linked bank accounts, 4 of which are closed. Firstly, online chat is soooooo slow. Minutes pass between each message while they deal with multiple chats at the same time. Not really a 'chat' service. It makes you lose the will to live. Secondly, you can't remove any bank account once it's connected, even if it no longer exists. This means you can easily withdraw funds to a closed account. I supposed you'd get your funds back eventually but Zopa won't allow you to remove any linked bank accounts ever. Utterly frustrating and pointless.

title: I received an invitation to join the…
review: I received an invitation to join the Beta Bank account and decided to give it a try. Opening the account was straightforward, and everything seemed fine at first. However, when I tried to set up a direct debit, enticed by the promise of cashback I ran into problems.The direct debit setup failed with the provider I was switching to showing the error message "CONTACT YOUR BANK – UNABLE TO VERIFY." I reached out to Zopa for assistance and was told they would investigate but I received no follow-up.Four weeks later, I tried again to set up the direct debit only to encounter the same issue and error message. When I contacted Zopa via chat the first agent joined and immediately left. I was then passed from person to person only to be told that they were aware "some customers experience direct debit issues."Frankly, it’s baffling that they advertise a current account without ensuring basic functionality like setting up direct debits works properly. A product like this should either be fully operational or not launched at all. As it stands the account is not fit for purpose and I wouldn’t recommend attempting to migrate any banking activity to them.

title: Very helpful staff members
review: I was having great difficulty with online banking when trying to make a time-sensitive arrangement. I had been booked in for a meeting on Friday, but the situation could not wait. I requested a phone-call and Rachel Allen from the Bolton branch (whom the appointment Friday was with) called me back promptly, and spent some time trying to fix my account so I could proceed online. Having fixed the issues, I no longer needed to wait five days to make the arrangement, and could proceed myself simply and quickly. I have high praise for all of her help.  I would like to add that having been with TSB for some time, the online banking services are far behind other banks, but it's more than made up for by the call centre staff. Virtually no wait times, and always very helpful and pleasant.

At the end I was very happy with it as I promptly repaid it thanks to good investment advice and now I am debt free. I want to thank the bank for giving me the loan. title: Getting the important things done when it is busy
review: Even though it has been a busy time with the move not running smoothly, I really appreciated that my mortgage advisor, Pius, followed up with me to make sure I had put appropriate Home Contents, Life Insurance and Critical Illness Cover in place for my new flat and mortgage. When there are so many things to set up and get done, it is easy to forget these things. Pius made the process straight forward, clear, brought my details up to date and I got the insurance that I needed for peace of mind for my home and my money. Great service, Great products.

title: My Savings Account moving issues:
review: I had some issues trying to move our savings account which was due to expire. I thought it would be a simple matter of choosing and moving from one to the new account with a better rate. I had issues signing in on Internet banking because the App wouldn’t allow me to do that. Clicking the Apply Now button didn’t work.I had to completely re-apply for Internet Banking and then I discovered I couldn’t open the App so I hadto re-apply for that too. It would have made sense to me at any rate if TSB had just moved my account to the better rate themselves having told me they would do that automatically unless I told them not to.

title: Switch service query
review: Called customer services with a query about switching service, on hold for a long time, when call was answered, despite selecting 'switch' option, I was advised the call had to be transferred to the switch team, after this happened the call was terminated as, it being before 9am they were not open !!. The initial call handler should have known this. On calling the dedicated switch team (after 9am) the representative was very helpful, if I had spoken to her initially it would have been a 5* review, but the 1st person, appeared to just want to move onto the next call asap. Card arrived promptly.

title: New Leytonstone branch
review: Service at the new local branch in Leytonstone is very hit and miss. I used to attend the Bethnal Green branch but that’s now closed. Since attending here the customer service is decided on who you see. I have attended here now a few times and there just seems to be one financial manager that seems to want to help, the other financial manager is always in the office with door closed and no customer.  Rahim attended to me  most recently when he saw me waiting in line because they have only one teller. Other advisors walked past on their cell phone without asking me if they can attend to me. He  resolved  my problem with knowledge so if you go to this branch ask for Mr Rahim he will help you gladly.

Finally gave up after waiting what seemed like an age. Have now managed to sort out my problem on my own. In the past I would go to the Winslow TSB Bank and talk to someone. Now I have to go to Milton Keynes where they are always very helpful.

title: Dealing with a blocked card
review: My debit card was blocked during an attempt to pay my plumber by PayPal on line. After my phone call to TSB had been on hold for over 20 minutes it was answered but on a poor quality connection. TSB ended the call after a few seconds before I was able to explain my problem. This was incredibly frustrating! That afternoon I visited TSB's Archway Branch, where a very helpful and friendly advisor was able to reactivate my card and arrange the transfer.

title: Mixed experience
review: The lady who helped us to open our joint account was superb, she explained everything and was lovely. However, this appointment was rearranged due to staff illness the week prior and we lost out on the switching bonus. Nobody contacted us to inform us our initial appointment could not go ahead so we didn't find out until we attended branch. At this point we made it clear we only wanted an appointment that allowed us to get the switching bonus and were assured we would get this. When we attended our second appointment, the woman told us the switching bonus had ended the 6th Sept and she was very sorry but we wouldn't be able to get it. Very disappointing as we had planned to switch our current accounts also but didn't as a result of this and our initial appointment was on the 7th so we should have been informed at that stage.

title: I was cut off twice just when I got…
review: I was cut off twice just when I got through to the fraud team, and I have been on hold for 35 minutes and counting. I am calling from Australia (in the evening) as I have a power of attorney for my mother's account and need to do some banking on her behalf, which I am legally set up to do. The POA has been verified by TSB, which was a long process as I needed a UK address, but we got there. Transacting on my mother's account is entirely different, as the first time I used the banking app, the fraud team locked me out.  It is very frustrating and time-consuming so far...please TSB make it easier to interact with you.

description: TSB is a retail bank with a trusted customer brand, heritage stretching back to the start of the savings bank movement 200 years ago, and a committed workforce that offer full service banking to more than five million customers. We operate on a modern banking platform and serve our customers through digital channels, over the phone and in branches across the UK.
title: Branch service declined significantly
review: Unfortunately, after 11 years of being very happy with the Aberystwyth TSB branch, I can no longer offer a more than 2 stars review. Whereas before they offered competitive services or rates, they are no longer standing out from the rest of the banks - or rather, in some areas they stand out by performing worse.Firstly, they no longer offer in-branch mortgage service. I spoke to an advisor in central office on the phone, who was very polite and promised a call back - but noone phoned me. The counter windows are permanently covered with blinds nowadays which gives off an impression that the branch is in the process of being closed. I ended up taking my (re)mortgage business elsewhere.Secondly, unlike other banks who offer an alternative use of an authentication device that looks like a calculator, TSB only allow you to log in if you have a working mobile phone. A deal breaker for those living in rural areas where the signal is patchy, or that, most recently, had no mobile service for a whole week due to storms.And thirdly, I had some old-style pound coins that came from my washing machine. They were dented and battered, so I came into the branch to ask staff if they'd exchange them for new coins, and the advisor said they would, but they've already closed the vault, so I needed to come back another day. When I came in on another day, another advisor was adamant that the coins were "fake". It was a very poor choice of words; the coins were obviously real just really battered, and he had no means of telling that they were "fake". If anything, he coud have said: "I'm sorry, I can't be certain that they are real". So again, I took them to another bank, who changed them without any questions asked. TSB have just lost a loyal customer because of £2.I'll be sticking around with TSB until a switch deal comes along for another bank, but unfortunately, its days of being a competitive customer focused business that I valued are gone.ADDED following TSB response: thanks very much, James. I will of course consider TSB again in the future when my mortgage comes up for renewal, and I hope this will give it enough time to impove the inbranch service. As I said, I couldn't fault the service up until Covid, with late opening hours, Saturdays service and mortgage advisor in branch. In the days when everything is on-line, it was a great incentive to remain with TSB even when a better financial products were available elsewhere.

title: Techology Improving Things??
review: I use my internet banking at home. When I go into the branch I would hope to get help not just do the same thing I can at home. ie logging onto my account via my phone or app where the staff can not apparently make adjustment to acounts as they used to! I was told only a very limited number of staff are 'trained/ qualified' to do this now?It was very frustrating and time wasting as after two visits to my branch and two phone calls to customer services, in the end having to post my instruction in two separate (prepaid) envelopes to the same place! Feeling it would have been helpful to have had more support/advice regarding my banking in branch.Even felt inclined to change banks, though I've been loyal with TSB for many years, not wanting to do so. Told that all banks are the same these days and probably are! Technology suposedly improvement sadly I do not agree. Although I must add all the staff I have spoken to were polite and friendly even if the could not help.

title: Customer servives AWFUL
review: Reviews about good customer service have to be faked. Bank wise, it’s a bank, it’s fine it works, but if you EVER need to contact them about anything at all you’ll have the worst experience ever. The staff when you call up are untrained and just read from scripts, they repeat the same thing over and over again and have accents that are very unintelligible which is very unhelpful. The only time you speak to a helpful kind person if is you go through to the complaints department which is very telling. I’ve been with them for years and years and every time I need to talk to them I have an awful time, I’ve finally moved banks and getting them to delete me off their system is proving very difficult, they don’t respond quickly and leave you on hold / waiting on chat while you ‘go to a different team’ honestly the care is just atrocious, my whole family have had same experiences and are now moving too. Just don’t bother. Go to a different bank. Save yourself the bother

title: Consistently appalling
review: Every single time I've had to call them (6 X in less than one year) it's taken at least 12 minutes to get a reply, to spend 10 minutes explaining the query (having made this same call again and again), to then be transferred to a colleague and have to wait another minimum 12 minutes - then to them be disconnected!  Calling again, asking for option to make complaint, to take this time 18 minutes to get response.  And the online chat option is abysmal.  Hugely frustrating, poorest customer service I've experienced in my life.  I have suffered them for less than a year, and will be looking for another bank pronto! NB one star is too many.  The staff, whilst pleasant enough, don't seem bothered when you tell them what happened.  That must speak volumes of the culture.

title: TSB A BIG WAIST OF TIME
review: I spent hours with a member of the staff at the Hereford Branch of TSB entering all my details on line to open a business account for the Ukraine Freedom Co (Herefordshire) I opened a private account.I returned twice to the branch with extra ID and documents. Then my request was submitted, I then received an e-mail informing me my application had been turned down, with no explanation. I contacted the TSB helpline and was informed that TSB do not deal with not for profit organizations. This was all a big waist of my time, and I not interested in opening the private account. I was going to close down the two accounts I have and transfer the fund to the new accounts, but this has all been a big waist of time.

title: Dreadful experience
review: Dreadful service - My bank account was blocked on the 20th December with no prior warning or notification. The staff at TSB stated they didn't know why as there were no notes on my record and the ''blocking department'' do not take calls from customers. I have been without any money, as this is my only bank account, all over Xmas to now with no sign of a resolution. Appalling way to treat a customer and shocking lack of customer service to leave me destitute and not able to draw on MY own money. TSB should be ashamed of themselves !

title: TSB and fraud suspicion. Law unto themselves
review: I have been a TSB customer since 2001 but today i removed almost every penny from that bank.THREE times in 10 days they have blocked my account and cancelled my cards because they claim that buying presents at Xmas is unusual. 4 bloomin hours I have had to spend on the phone to them in total. Absolutely ridiculous. None of my other banks have done anything similar despite similar or even more spending. The TSB seem to think our time is free, not offering compensation for mistakes made - or even any apology. Sick of it so I wont be using the TSB again other than to pay any direct debits due out. Its MY money NOT yours so stop behaving like it is yours.

title: Zero response to my complaint.
review: Having had my account blocked for no reason  anyone at the TSB could explain to me, I spent several hours and calls, phoning their customer services to try and get my account unblocked. Following this, I had to make two visits to my local branch to try and sort it out with confusing results. The staff who I spoke to didn't seem to know what the other staff were already doing. The entire experience was quite draining, and to add insult to injury the TSB returned my pension to source, which meant I had to borrow a thousand pounds to cover my D/D 's. I have received zero response to my complaint. Zilch. Absolutely nothing. And TSB, if you can be bothered to respond to this poor review, then please don't ask me to 'follow this link' etc. I only have a few years left and I don't want to waste any more of my precious time, chasing the TSB!

title: ACCOUNT REACTIVATION FIASCO
review: ACCOUNT REACTIVATION FIASCO. I have already been  offered compensation on 2 complaints about this  (ref 10090815)  but still have an inactive account and can't access my money.  I would just like the account closed and a cheque sent to the address you hold for me.   GRRRRRRR    STEVEN.     RE response below.   Sorry Stacey but this highlights the staff knowledge problem.   Someone senior tried to close the account for me.  You can't close it while inactive!  Might be worth checking the complaint number referenced before responding.. this just adds to my deep frustration with TSB.

title: I attempted an online purchase and TSB…
review: I attempted an online purchase and TSB blocked my card. I did get texts asking if it was me making the purchase and even though I replied “Yes” it remained blocked.So I called the bank and the operator told me it he would unblock it - but no, it remained blocked until I got ANOTHER text saying to get in touch. Then I had to answer lots of questions about my account before it was finally unblocked and I could purchase my new camera.I appreciate the need for caution and security in case of fraud but my card should have been unblocked at the first text asking if it was me attempting a purchase

title: From 5* to no stars
review: Recently gave 5* review. However now find my card blocked. Took 30 mins to eventually get out through to fraud dept who sent email form to fill in and gave me number to call back. Cant get through on the number they gave me - option of having received email not available. So currently cant use card. Eventually got use of card back after waiting 45 minutes for phone to be answered.  No explanation of why card was blocked. And agent told me to call back as if it were a straightforward thing to do. Also process where I had to send photo of passport over complicated. All in all over 2 hours waiting or answering inane questions from each person spoken to when trying to resolve. Can no longer rely on card and have had to get backup card with other bank. And no explanation is just awful customer service.I've now looked at other recent reviews and looks like TSB have a serious systematic problem in blocking accounts for no good reason. And their standard response is "We’re sorry your experience didn’t match your expectations." Note the weasel words which translate to being sorry customers expect a reasonable service.

title: Every little help HELPS! Helpers being present fantastic
review: I stupidly double clicked on my phone to pay 1p for a document I was expecting to download for free. I was in a hurry, flustered and not thinking. Next thing I am told that I have subscribed to a company for 24 hours after which I will be charged £49.95 pcm to be able to read all sorts of stuff. OUCH.The payment was to be made from my Tesco credit card. It’s Boxing Day! Very late in the afternoon! Help!! Surely Tescobank wont be manned to deal with this sort of issue.OMG they were “manned” - Thank you Deborah for your availability, and for walking me through the whole process a) of undoing the mess I created for myself; b) explaining that I could have my card stopped for a few days to prevent new payment requests; c) have the “dodgy” supplier placed on a permanent STOP LIST to my account; d) request a replacement card. Deborah was very supportive and Tesco by their presence EVEN ON BOXING DAY earn the full 5 STARS.My peace of mind instantly restored - Thank you.

title: More Guidance Needed
review: Being able to manage my account online. Easy Navigation.I did experience some initial difficulty in managing my e-saver which had reached maturity. I was advised via letter that I could give instructions on line as to either renewing, transferring or closing my e-saver. I searched and tried to find in 'manage my account' how to give my instructions but the system did not support this. After much frustration I realised to continue to reinvest my e-saver I would need to open a new e-saver and opt for; transfer of savings from the matured account to the new one. This method, as far as I could see was not explained in the letter nor online.I was looking for a drop down box that would give choices re: options at maturity. I had to rely on my own initiative to solve the conundrum. I believe that every procedure should be thought through, from the perspective of how easy is our system to navigate, and does it provide sufficient information to guide the customer through each process.Informing a customer that their maturing e-saver can be managed online is insufficient, and needs specific details on exactly how this is done.

review: I am a fan of Tesco’s. The staff of the local High Street store here in Lymington work their socks off to provide a good service in their cramped floor space, and the hypermarket at Milton-on-Sea is an excellent store for car users. As an old person, I find all the offers and bonuses too confusing, and wish you would just do what M&S does occasionally - just knock some money off my bill!

Reason for decline well I don’t earn enough money apparently wellIf so how my own bank gave me loan with 13% apr but u gave me beginning of form 8.9% ? ! My score is ok so got my loan from my bank all reason was me trying to get fairer rate else where when I searched for better rates and come  a cross Tesco plus having lower rate being Tesco club card so guess what it’s all trick to harvest my personal data to sell third parties which they all got my details now thanks to tesco  every little helps ! No explanation given other than that the information I have during application was inaccurate. The questions were not clear as it asked about contributions to your mortgage. This was explained much better by my own bank online where they were offering me 3 times the loan I required. Only applied for Tesco loan via club card discount as it was slightly cheaper than my own bank. Feel I have wasted my time and it may have affected my perfect credit rating. I was left feeling ck fused and annoyed and thought that the Barclays Bank takeover  may have had something to do with it. Won’t ever consider Tesco bank in future

title: Should be a dedicated callback line for fraud checks
review: Using Tesco credit card for big purchases only which I am confident to pay/clear in full every month. Not using this card for regular use due to high interest rate.Downside of using card for large purchase triggers their fraud checks. Even though you have cleared MFA checks, they will ask endless questions to be certain. But wasted my almost half an hour on the callback number to their contact centre which then passed me to specialist team asking same security questions again which I cleared already. Ideally they should have 1 dedicated fraud callback to avoid wasting time.

title: I thought Tesco Bank would be a safe…
review: I thought Tesco Bank would be a safe bet. How wrong was I! Don’t make the mistake of paying your credit card off in full when you get paid, thinking you can still access that money if you get short of cash later in the month - no - Tesco will not allow you to withdraw any cash at all. The most galling thing is, is that they do this without telling you. Absolutely shocking way to treat customers. Oh and all the inconvenience, stress and embarrassment of not being able to withdraw available cash is ‘to help me’?? I’m still trying to get my head around that. Disgusting way to treat customers.

description: Tesco Bank offers a wide range of everyday banking products including loans, credit cards, savings and Clubcard Pay+.  Take advantage of our special offers and earn Clubcard points. Find out more today!
title: Tescobank dishonesty! Applied for a 6% loan, got an offer at 18%
review: Applied for a loan online and was quoted a 6% approx interest rate, made an application, it was accepted but then the paperwork came through with a near 20% interest rate. Did they think I wouldn't notice and would just sign or something? It's so dishonest, borderline fraudulent and I will also be looking at options for formal complaints. I was told over the phone the online rate is just the lowest possible you could get, not an accurate representation. I'm staggered that they're allowed to get away with such a ridiculous and dishonest approach to their loans. Have since reported their bait and switch tactics to the FCA, Tescobank can defend itself in comments here, I doubt the regulator will take a positive view of them though.

title: Worse customer advice ever
review: I've had my credit card since approx. 2007 - i've have great credit consistently for years. I don't know what idiot suddenly made a mess at this business, but it went from okay to a massive pile of shite. In all fairness, if they ever cares at all they would have suggested to me sooner to waive the debt at a smaller amount. But after awful advice by a false charity that isn't worth a penny to even sit and listen to about something i knew considerably more about, the stupid tesco staff keep suggesting some different bullshit charity when they should just do the thing i can ask perfectly fine directly by myself. Never use the 'breath space' it's a load of false bullshit because they still use wording like 'interest' on a no interest hold. I've written and phoned to have my account balance cleared by waive (also known as write-off) and still havent had a response. The amount is simply too high for me to pay anything on, but so low an amount it wouldn't bother tesco to just do the decent solid thing and delete the account entirely. I shouldn't have been issued a credit card!! Tesco gave it out like it was candy and i didn't even have a job when someone else pressured me to apply for it one. There's a lot of horrible people in the world and i'd like to pass that debt right on back to all of those that even tried for a second to have me have it instead. There never needed be a god for justice.

Hopefully I'll be accessing the account within the next 24hrs.

title: Technical Issue over a week now
review: UPDATE 05/12/24 issue was resolved in less than 24 hours after this post and someone from the tide escalation team called me and apologised. Thanks for the quick response team tide! I applied for a business account over a week ago and waited for approval. After several days I got a call that there is a technical issue, the support agent apologised and said they have already escalated the issue and it should be all resolved within the next 24 hours. That call is now 6 days ago, I just called the service team again and nothing has moved, nor could they advice me. I'd really like to use Tide, but if an experience already starts off like mine I will look into a different provider.

title: This bank is ok for a small business…
review: This bank is ok for a small business bank. However if you make large payments frequently then look elsewhere. Constantly I have payments that are held impeding my ability to conduct business in a timely fashion. This 2024 not 1833..... I leave messages via the contact chat for it to stay dormant for hours. Not great when suppliers are holding stock to be released.Please can Tide sort this out. Update: after this review it was sorted and the payments were cleared.

title: A few things of concern for small businesses like myself.
review: I recently opted for tide business account, however after reading reviews around accounts being frozen for no reason and support lacking  it’s not something a small business like my own could survive for weeks with funds frozen. I think it’s similar to PayPal holding funds without reason, and it’s really worried me that there is no telephone option to speak to an actual person. I will say my experience so far 1st month has been very straightforward and way better than Starling etc, I cannot understand why it uses your personal name instead of your business name when paying and giving customers your details and customers asking if it’s Clearpay ? And not Tide it’s a little confusing in that department too.

title: Be prepared for frustrations

Customer services don’t deal with this, their specialist team only communicates via the messaging service, once provided everything (which would not break GDPR/Data protection - which they seem to not worry about) I am still waiting. The chat ended with the worker saying if there was anything else to contact them, 3 messages later and still no response.

title: Easy to open an account but wouldn't recommend…
review: Easy to open an account and app is good. BUT if you ever experience and problems or have any questions, their support chat is absolutely diabolical. I cannot access two of my three accounts at present, due to a change they have made with their upgraded design and I have been waiting for over 8 hrs for a reply. My last issue which was early 2024, was questioning why I kept receiving random messages from their support chat. They admitted there was a problem their end and 'apparently' had a team of specialists working on it to rectify the problem (obviously lies). It took several months to get sorted. Pretty much the only problem I have with them is the reaction and response to customer problems, but this is a huge fail and would prevent me ever recommending them.

title: Tide Bank, Poor Quality Customer Service
review: My Business Acct has been open for over a year, when everything is going well Tide is fine but the App Based approach makes it difficult to resolve issues effectively as Tide Bank is by no means proactive in communicating.1) During the verification process of transferring a large amount of money £XXX,XXX to another bank, the App did not recognise the genuine Sort Code and corrected me, causing me to add the wrong Payee to my Account and encouraging me to override the checks, l nearly sent the money to wrong bank.2) The App still doesn't recognise the Account details entered, which l have verified myself independently as genuine from Google.3) The Transfer has still not been made nearly 2 weeks after the request Tide Bank has been sitting on my £XXX,XXX and l am unable to reverse the payment via the App. 4) I have been in contact Tide Bank 4 times as l was instructed via email, the issue has yet to be resolve, no telephone calls or emails.5) I felt obliged to take time warn other Customers of the likely issues with this App Based Bank.
