title: YOUR MONEY IS NOT SAFE HERE
review: YOUR MONEY IS NOT SAFE HERE! Tide do not take your security seriously.I will be closing my account with Tide due to the recent encounter my girlfriend has faced with her Tide account.She is a small business owner. Tide allowed a hacker to access her account and take two unauthorised payments totalling over £550.After which, the scammer called her pretending to be Tide to help. Due to the initial payments that had just come out, he convinced her to secure her compromised account by approving a pop up in the app. They emptied the account which was thousandsShe never would have engaged with the secondary scam on the phone had Tide protected her account and blocked the hacker taking the first two payments. It is a total breach of securityFurthermore, Tide have not only refused to help financially, they have victim blamed, made countless mistakes and inconsistencies in their findings, written very lazy correspondences and have shown absolutely no careWe are completely horrified that their security is that easily compromised and that the customer care has been so poor. I’ll be closing my account, it’s not worth the riskWe have since been contacted by an internationally renowned journalist who has offered to run a story on what has happened as it may help others in the future.

title: Complete Disaster – Avoid at All Costs
review: Opening a business account with Tide has been one of the worst decisions I’ve made as a business owner. Despite being approved, less than a month in, my account was suddenly “temporarily paused,” leaving me completely unable to make payments, transfer funds, or even accept payments. They claimed this would take up to 20 days to resolve, but here I am over a month later, with no resolution and endless excuses.Tide’s customer service is beyond incompetent. Every inquiry is met with vague, robotic responses, blaming FCA regulations or extending their already unacceptable time frame due to “further steps.” Two weeks ago, I was told my case was escalated, but absolutely nothing has happened since. To make matters worse, I was told that simply asking for updates could cause further delays. It’s clear they have no urgency, accountability, or regard for their customers’ businesses.This is not a business bank—it’s a liability. With only a handful of transactions under £250, there is absolutely no reason for this chaos. Tide has proven to be completely unfit to handle business banking, not to mention the disruption.Stay far away from Tide unless you enjoy frustration, excuses, and having your business held hostage. This bank is an utter disgrace.

All I wanted to do was store £10,000-£20,000 for the interest rate. If I’m already trading why would I be setting up a business savings account to trade out of? I already have a reliable bank for that.I wanted to question this, but Tide seemed to have blocked me from logging in and opening a live chat. I went into the website and they’ve made it impossible to contact them without logging in, which I can’t due to Tides poor system. Due to the difficulty of contacting them it worries me what might happen if sometime were to go wrong and they had my money. Keep away in my opinion. Might be ok now…but if something goes wrong they don’t seem to easy to contact.

title: Pretty Decent So Far

Never had any issues, paperwork always turned up as promised, online account access is always fine, website is fine, and never had to wait long on few occassions I needed to telephone customer support (which clearly sounds based in UK).They dont often offer the absolute top interest rates but always seem up there with the best on many occassions.Only negative is I've been unable so far to persuade them to consider a reward for introducing a new customer to invest with them.

title: My father has just died
review: My father has just died and has an account with you - I'm saddened that you're one of the old fashioned companies that requires a copy of grant of probate, knowing that this is going to take many months to obtain, when the money is his, or his estate's.  Most other companies just ask for a death certificate and proof of executors.  This is just prolonging the difficult time we are already suffering and it is unnecessary.  Shame, as I'd always thought highly of Aldermore, would consider whether to use them again or not in the future.

It's incredibly frustrating, the website is very basic and problematic. Having said that, whenever I've spoken to the customer service team they are lovely and helpful.

title: Poor conduct and attitude to customers
review: Good rates, ok website, beware of poor conduct and attitude to customers. e.g.1. Had a savings account for 4+ years. Then received a phishing like email (worded like it came from scammers) asking for confidential data and threatening to freeze account. 5 days later account frozen, and refused to re-open it, even after clearing security.  Reason: Aldermore changed some account requirement that urgently needed some extra data, then mismanaged the comms and so only way to access money was to close the account.e.g.2. Account opening issues. Much pain and effort because my wife's surname has a hyphen and one ID from an utility company showed it without. An already laborious process took 6 weeks to resolve. Got £75 compensation both times, but they took no action to improve their processes/attitude.

title: Poor communication and unprofessionalism mortgage application
review: Mortgage Application and communication. Very poor. It’s sad I have to write an poor review but this is honest and true.I’ve applied for a mortgage application via Aldermore.Valuation done 24/04/23. Everything moving smooth. Then boom. A technical fault with my application, the rectified that. It is now JUNE. My offer is still not handed to me. Even though I’ve been told several times “last stages of underwriting” I’ve been told 2 working days, 5 working days etc etc. This was via my broker. I’ve seen other peoples reviews when it comes to mortgages. Sadly my experience is as bad as there’s. It’s true. The underwriting process is very slow and unprofessional due to lack of communication and breaking promise when it comes to deadlines.I was called last week by an Aldermore employee, who apologies for the delay etc and told me my application is basically done. Just waiting to be signed off, after they get them some bits. I sent them bits and still not contact from them or my mortgage broker. It’s sad as this has caused me unnecessary stress and anxiety. I thought highly of Aldermore, but what turned out to be so promising is turning out to a disappointing experience.

title: A little Sympathy and Humanity
review: I held two accounts with you: one, the 2-yr Fixed and a double Access until September last year.When I opened the Fixed-term deposit and for the cooling-off period in April/ May 2022, the interest rates offered by you were probably one of the best around. Then, suddenly, after the cooling period, the interest rates shot up, making the 1.93% that you were giving a joke.In many messages, I pointed out to you that I was disabled and had been unemployed since my diagnosis in June 2018 and that the difference in interest rates amounted to about £300 a month. With my future employment prospects looking grim, I asked very politely that Aldermore be a bit flexible in view of my situation and that I simply could not afford to lose £300 a month.You wrote back very coldly and unsympathetically that, to avoid destitution, I could access my Double Access. That was hardly the point.Because of this, I have lost 24 x £300 = £7200. This is money that I need for the future. I have been advised to find alternate accommodation with the progressive quality of my illness.Of course, as a private financial institution, you stood on the contract I signed before the interest rates went up. I asked you to show some flexibility to the situation. You showed none. I asked you to show some signs of sympathy and humanity: you showed none.Now that the fixed-term deposit has reached maturity, I feel I don't want to invest with Aldermore any longer.In fact, I'm thinking of posting this scenario on TrustPilot and recommendation websites.

After almost a week I received a phone call from Aldemore saying that they needed documentary evidence of the emergency before further consideration could be given. I refused to send anything further as this was a private matter. How heartless can this be when you cannot access your own savings for a genuine reason.Avoid Aldemore at all costs

title: Very deceitful
review: Absolutely shocking company. I rejected a vehicle and it took them 3 months to sort out. No one knows what the other is doing, they contradict themselves all the time. I had 3 different people from Aldermore advising me that I would receive all my money back. They collected the vehicle and only made a partial refund so make sure you get everything in writing.They discriminate against people who pay smaller deposits and higher repayments. They reference different sections of the Consumer Credit Act 2015 to try and deflect the blame on to the consumer. One of the complaints department is very abrasive and rude, constantly talking over you. If you do take out an agreement of any kind, make sure you have the funds to employ a lawyer so that you get all of what you are owed. My lawyer has now written to Aldermore as their policy is flawed. In an email they also confirmed that they not advise on their “‘usage policy as it can lead to misuse “ UPDATE - Aldermore’s Response “As to the mention of disclosure, we are not looking to introduce contractual terms into the agreement after we have entered into it, rather looking to rely upon our legal rights afforded to us under the Consumer Rights Act.  We believe that we have acted fairly and reasonably in our conduct in compliance with regulatory expectations, and that the deduction of four monthly payments for 5,852 mileage is a good outcome for you.”So there idea of 7 trips to Travel World,  a total of 21 days in repair and 1600miles in fuel and “it’s a good outcome for me” Motor Finance commission payments ?

title: Bad Ltd Co mortgage admin
review: I have a business mortgage for my rental property held in a Ltd company; used Aldermore (two year) tracker.  It may be because it is not as regulated, as a company versus an individual, they seem to be able to act unfairly at will. I thought that when the rates fell, it would track as swiftly as when they went up and by the same amount - makes sense, right?  "Tracker..."But no, an extra month at the old rate.  And only down by 0.2%, not the 0.25%.  Unreal.  I wonder what the rate will drop by after the change three weeks ago, when and if at all.All I got as a response was "When the base rate changes it can take some time to process the change, especially if our systems or policies involve administrative delays. I can see you are on Aldermore Manage rate."Perhaps they are better with the more regulated personal products, as they have a reasonable score here.  Staff seem nice on the phone.It's probably somewhere in the small-print that they can delay the rate drops and not reflect the amounts dropped.  You'd like to think these companies could be trusted.

title: Awful experience with mortgage application with Aldermore!
review: Currently going through a mortgage application and it has been an awful experience with Aldermore. You are on the phone for a long time .. SLA keeps on changing from one person you speak to and another. And up till now, after waiting for 7 days for even a review only to be told today that it is now 9 days before a review. This has been going on for over 7 weeks now. Worst experience ever! I have other experiences with other banks which have been pleasant , but experience has been frustrating with Aldermore. Even now , not even sure what will happen after the 9 days review.i cannot wait for this nightmare of customer service to end!

title: Poor Mortgage Process
review: Can’t understand why they are getting good reviews here as they are rubbish on every other site/search. Obviously they are happy to take peoples savings but thats about it.They have absolutely no interest in customer service. Dragging out a mortgage application which has left me to lose the house because its been over 4 months and they just respond when my broker chases them and even then they just ask a question that has been already answered. I spent £2k messing around and getting no answers, its appalling and they should be ashamed that all they ask people to do is email them with feedback with no end goal. They couldn't care less even if they triedQuick update: 25/10 sent them details as soon as I got the “so sorry” reply. Emailed said busy but will respond within 48hrs…96hrs(4 days) later still nothing. Classic, avoid them if you can help it!

title: AVOID AT ALL COSTS
review: AVOID AT ALL COSTSAVOID AT ALL COSTS – SHOCKING SERVICE FROM ALDERMORE BANK. I WISH THERE WAS A -100 STARAVOID AVOID AVOID please for your own mental wellbeing don't approach this bank.I strongly urge everyone to AVOID dealing with Aldermore for mortgage applications. I started my application in August 2024. The property valuation was completed within a week, but from that point on, Aldermore went completely silent. Despite countless follow-ups from my broker, they kept insisting that the application was "with the underwriter."Fast forward to the beginning of October, they finally informed my broker that an offer had been made, yet Aldermore still hasn’t released the official offer letter. It’s now been nearly 11 weeks since the process began! The sheer lack of communication and accountability is absolutely unacceptable.Aldermore, you should be ashamed of yourselves. This is no way to treat customers. Avoid this awful bank at all costs if you value your time and sanity!

title: HSBC ON BNS SAVER
review: My experience with the HSBC ON BNS Saver savings account was excellent, and it quickly became my preferred online savings option. A key highlight was the 4% interest rate. While many banks drastically reduced their rates, HSBC maintained this high rate, which appeals to savers like myself.However, I recently learned that HSBC plans to reduce the interest rate. Given that the 4% rate is still available, I’m taking advantage of it. It feels like a smart move to make the most of this opportunity before the rate changes!

title: I was horrified to discover an…
review: I was horrified to discover an unauthorised direct debit on my church's bank account today.   I'm the treasurer.   We don't ever use direct debits on the bank account in question.   However, I telephoned the fraud department on the number shown on HSBC website, and Dhruvin cancelled it efficiently and politely.      It is very scary that a fraudster could so easily set up a direct debit on our account.  Perhaps HSBC needs to review what information it requires when setting up direct debits.   Our church has a rule that any two of four signatories must authorise payments.  How could someone by-pass this?

title: Complaints Dept.
review: Had occasion to be transferred from, presumably, the overseas call centre, to the complaints dept which seemed to be UK based.  Once there, received excellent service, with a return phone call to try and sort out a difficult problem of a lost account.  They got there in the end and the agent was very thorough, courteous and efficient.  I was surprised by an offer of "a gesture of goodwill" to be paid into my account.  And I don't even bank with HSBC! So it's not all bad.

review: I lost my debit card on Monday, August 14 and reported this and applied for a new card on the same day via the phone app. I wanted to transfer funds to another bank acct. (not HSBC) but kept receiving the error message saying payee details could not be confirmed. Popped into the Camden town branch and the extremely helpful team member organised the transfer for me and confirmed the new card was in my Apple Wallet. Received a text on August 15 saying my replacement card was on its way and it arrived safely next day! Banks receive such bad press, but HSBC has always been professional and if anything goes wrong, very speedy at fixing the problem. I just hope they continue to keep local branches open... the missing star was due to lack of clarity during a phone call re card replacement.

title: Initially attracted by the Martin Lewis…
review: Initially attracted by the Martin Lewis information, but was interested in their currency card, although Ive not got as far as that yet!Opening account is fine, a lot of emails and letters but ok. Transferring money in to the current account is easy, as is spending it. However, trying to get some of it back out (into the same source account)....oh dear. A fraud text, three phone calls, 5 different operators. Strangest questions, I appreciate a level of security but this was another level, I actually thought I had dialled a scam number and they were phishing for information........................................................Update, after 9 months I have found the currency card to be extremely useful. Several trips to Euro and non Euro countries have been made much easier with the card.

title: Formal Complaint Regarding Unprofessional Customer Service
review: Subject: Formal Complaint Regarding Unprofessional Customer ServiceDear  HSBC Ipswich I am writing to formally express my disappointment with the way I was treated by one of your staff members, Phil, during my visit to your branch on December 30, 2024. The incident left me feeling upset and disrespected, and I believe it highlights a need for better training on how to handle sensitive situations.To provide context, my husband, Patrick, has entrusted me with managing his financial affairs due to his health challenges over the past five years. Despite my encouragement for him to handle these matters directly, he has requested that I speak on his behalf as he finds it difficult to process such discussions.During my visit, Phil asked Patrick about the nature of certain transactions. When I explained the situation and answered his questions, he repeatedly avoided making eye contact with me and directed his responses to my husband instead. When I encouraged him to speak with Patrick directly, his reply, “but you’re the one talking and not Patrick, is it,” felt dismissive and unprofessional. Phil then took Patrick aside for a private conversation, leaving me feeling excluded and undervalued despite my genuine efforts to provide clear and honest information.While I understand that it is the bank’s responsibility to safeguard its customers, I believe the situation could have been handled with far greater sensitivity and professionalism. Managing my husband’s finances is already a challenging and emotionally taxing responsibility, and this experience added unnecessary stress to an already difficult situation.For 15 years, I have been financially independent, and stepping into this role was a decision made out of necessity due to my husband’s health. I am deeply committed to ensuring his well-being, and I expected your staff to show understanding and empathy during this interaction.I hope you will take this feedback seriously and provide training for your team to better handle similar situations in the future. Customers managing financial matters on behalf of loved ones often face unique challenges and deserve to be treated with dignity and respect.Thank you for taking the time to address this matter. I look forward to your response and to hearing how you plan to prevent similar experiences for other customers.Sincerely,Pamela Naylor

title: Would avoid
review: Never really had much of an issue with HSBC , only 3 stars since their branches are now kind of pointless . What’s the point in having a bank branch if you cannot accept coins / have a cash desk you rely on the cash machines to take the money to deposit so if u have a note that’s damaged or folded loads you just get told to go post office . Most banks now have a little area for cash deposits or them machines that eat the change to be deposited but why have a ‘ cashless bank branch ‘ ? To interact with staff to get additional services , help with app or account is good . But if someone looses their card and needs cash well good luck getting it from the local branch .

Otherwise I was happy with their security protocols and felt safe having account with them.

title: Online banking set up delay
review: Got a credit card, 0% interest on purchases for 22 months, so far so good.However, to be able to manage any card of any type we obviously need online banking set up, and herein lies the problem. After 2 phone calls and a promise they have posted the required telephone banking details to enable me to set up online banking, I’m still waiting for the post with the details! But then if they used Royal Mail, I could be in for a long wait.Update: a third phone call and they were able to fully complete and verify my setup through to completion over the phone. The app is a bit of overkill considering I only have a credit card (it covers all their accounts).It works for verifying card purchases as it should, but one limitation is that it doesn't list pending transactions! So a purchase (on the credit card at least) only appears in the app once the merchant has fully completed the transaction.

title: Good Branch service - don't bother trying to call them
review: I had a lot interaction with HSBC when I managed   my now deceased Mum's account via a Power of Attorney. I found the staff in my local branch in Romford Market Place to be both available and helpful; they were also very friendly and understanding.There were occasions when the branch was busy and I had to wait, or when the staff member couldn't help with my problem and had to seek advice but, I have nothing but thanks for them.However, I'm sure that most of the questions I had and actions I needed to take could have been achieved over the phone and online rather than the several Branch visits I had to make.I cannot give my review more than 3 stars because HSBC simply do not answer their phones, and their online banking app lacks decent features.I called HSBC so may times. More than once I waited over 30 minutes. I never spoke to a human over the phone. Never.I cannot recommend HSBC and am thankful that I don't have an account with them

title: Mixed experiences of customer service
review: Firstly, the bad news. My wife and I have been Premier customers for a very long time, and used to think HSBC was at least a 4star bank. However, now HSBC have withdrawn the live chat function from desktop online banking, you just get a chatbot which is worse than useless,  apparently you have to go on to their banking app on your mobile to reach this facility. Now I don't want banking on my mobile phone, I am liable to forget to take it with me and what if it is lost or stolen? So if I or my wife want to deal with a human which happened recently we have to ring up, go through multiple choice menus and recorded messages at length and even  then no human wants to speak to you. The operative we eventually got to speak to promised to deal with the issue (frozen ISA account) but didn't. We went to our nearest branch (York) to see if they could sort the problem but they could only tell us to ring up! Going round in circles here. This one is still pending.Now the good news, when we had to sort out the finances of a dead relative, the branch in York did have an operative to deal with that which was done effectively. Edit 23rd Oct: After posting this review, and getting in touch with HSBC "Social Media" we finally got the ISA issue sorted. We had to go back to York to the branch (which was unbeknownst to us shut for refurb) and see a member of HSBC staff in the Hilton Hotel, who at last sorted the problem out. Apparently if you don't put money in a cash ISA for a full tax year it is frozen. Who knew? It is not exactly publicised. So a hassle to sort out a simple issue. Not shining Customer Service HSBC!

title: Just so, so slow!
review: Had a MyAccount and MySavings for my child. Advisors themselves we’re really friendly, although everything was so slow! When trying to open account in-branch, took about 45 minutes which is much longer than any other bank we’ve used. App was outdated and not great to use, but not the worst either. My son has standing orders setup for funds to go from his main current account with another provider to his savings, but for this to work, he had to transfer to the MyAccount first and then to the MySavings. HSBC we’re slow at one of these transactions which resulted in him going slightly overdrawn as he didn’t use his main MyAccount. Received a slightly agressive letter saying that he might be reported to credit agencies for going £5 overdrawn! Not great and slow service.

title: I had a very good customer service…
review: I had a very good customer service experience when I called to make an enquiry. The call was answered quickly and I spoke to a young man who took time to check the information he gave me was correct and he explained things I didn't understand. I decided to apply for a bank account online the day after. Since then it hasn't been so good. I received a text to inform me my application had been received and ten days later have had no updates at all. Their website prides itself on their digital and mobile services, implying this is much faster and improved from good old counter services. I am not impressed at all. Had I gone into a bank to apply I am sure it would have been dealt with efficiently. How hard is it to send an update text so at least I know I am not forgotten, so it seems I will have to chase them up for the privilege of allowing them to have my business. I am sick of these 'improvements' that are costly and result in far inferior services. I will try elsewhere if I can even find a bank with counter service. Once upon a time ordinary didn't need to have banks, we used cash, our spending was simple and private. How stupid of us to let that go.

Been told to just apply again which would mean another credit search which would impact my score so just going to save for a few months instead of going through this again with HSBC. My mortgage was easier to get than a £3k loan. Gave an extra star as the person on the phone was helpful.

title: Your Loss HSBC
review: I applied for a HSBC credit card online last week which was immediately approved. I received the card through the post, downloaded the HSBC app and activated my new card. So far so good….until today when I received a letter from HSBC stating that they had doubts that this was a genuine application and requiring me to go to a branch in person with my passport and proof of address. As HSBC have recently closed my local branch, I was not about to travel further afield to confirm my identity in person. I telephoned HSBC; the staff were perfectly polite but insistent that I should attend a branch to prove my identity. Whilst I appreciate the need to prevent fraud, there are easier ways to confirm identity in 2024 so account closed and I’ll take my custom elsewhere. Your loss HSBC.

title: Bad payment clearing times
review: The app itself is decent, it gives you everything you need. However, taking a full 24 hours to clear a faster payment that is coming from an account I use every month is a joke. I used to work in credit cards customer service so I know that faster payments often clear with 2 hours. This month I forgot to make my payment until the day it was due. I paid it just in time. Next day I get a letter and a text saying my account is in arrears and that I need to make a payment. HSBC mark my words, if I have a mark on my credit score over this you will be hearing my complaint. There is absolutely no good reason that a payment should take 24 hours to clear if its coming from the same source every single month.
texto: Bad payment clearing times The app itself is decent, it gives you everything you need. However, taking a full 24 hours to clear a faster payment that is coming from an account I use every month is a joke. I used to work in credit cards customer service so I know that faster payments often clear with 2 hours. This month I forgot to make my payment until the day it was due. I paid it just in time. Next day I get a letter and a text saying my account is in arrears and that I need to make a payment. HSBC mark my words, if I have a mark on my credit score over this you will be hearing my complaint. There is absolutely no good reason that a payment should take 24 hours to clear if its coming from the same source every single month.

title: Communication is a very definite weak…
review: Communication is a very definite weak point. The customer team are lovely and helpful, but they are limited as to what they can do with the underwriters. We have only discovered information about our mortgage application through talking to the customer team. There have been no emails, phone calls or other communications from HSBC. The process takes too long. We have regularly been told it will take 3 more days every week for the past 6 weeks. I just wish we could talk to someone and find out what is happening, but it appears nobody can do that!! If we weren’t so far into the process I would switch supplier.

The reason we went to Bank for took over 3 hours from leaving our home, it is quite ridiculous.  We could tell many a story about other problems we have when telephoning etc.,   Please HSBC start listening to your customers as your business would not run without them !

title: After 20 years of being a good…
review: After 20 years of being a good customer, having a credit score within top 25% of UK, I'm offered a £500 interest free overdraft to help with the cost of living.HSBC accept my application, then refuse to increase my overdraft to make any use of it, and the hard search they conduct drops my score by 50 points.That is a bad service HSBC.Online security can be a real nightmare to sort out if it goes wrong or they update their system.And finally the call centres they are used have terrible quality phone line connections and I struggle to understand the staff as they do me.We are a financial services nation that now outsources these services more and more often.

title: Trying to open a joint account with a…
review: Trying to open a joint account with a person who currently lives in the EU. Has been and continues to be a catastrophic disaster with repeated misinformation from branch staff, phone line staff and in fact, the complaints resolution team. Branches in Scotland not staffed and functioning to provide account opening in spite of being open and available and directed to app where it is NOT even possible. Told to do it on phone banking of the website but during the process this met an error as both not in U.K. or EU. Told I’ll get a call to open it on the telephone. Call never materialised. When outcome of complaint, a thorough review lacking and staff member hadn’t an understanding of the basic facts. Attitude poor and condescending and didn’t like being corrected. Just wants to close complaint. Not resolve the issue still outstanding. She stated had to be done in branch (not currently in U.K.) or on telephone. Confirmed again as per previous call that it could be done together on the phone. So as call not coming to us, we made another call only to be told it isn’t possible and has to be in branch. Customer service is dire and considering one of their staff mantras is JUST GET IT DONE! Well,…. What’s more troubling is they don’t seem to care. Business flying out the window and a review rating of over 80% agreeing with my one star, HSBC need to be sitting up and listening. Appalling and disgraceful.

title: Stressed me out to maximum yesterday
review: HSBC Stressed me out to maximum yesterday as HSBC has done previously with threats to close accounts after decades of Banking history... 2 hours trying to resolve the HSBC not understanding accurate information... To be compliant to their demands they compelled me to give security information passwords and my hand written signature to a third party agency namely Adobe. Com this sensitive information should only be lodged on a HSBC website platform... I have made an official complaint thats been recognised by HSBC... in these days of astronomic Internet fraud and very dangerous AI the bank should not stress their clients out this way... This disgraceful methodology is titled SAFEGUARD that basically is not in my opinion safe guarding me as we have moved 2 of our 4 commercial accounts to other banks... title: Useless Customer Service!
review: HSBC rejected an international wire payment I was sent but cant give me a reason why. They have verified and confirmed the details were all correct so there is no logical reason to reject it, yet they did. The funds have not been returned to the sender though, so the sender is asking for evidence it was rejected by HSBC and that they returned the funds. HSBC cannot do this... I just get passed from department to department on the phone but no one can help. No one can send me the evidence of the rejection as they are a telephone banking team so cant send emails, the online chat cant help as they don't deal with these types of queries and my local branch cant help as they don't deal with international payments! Absolutely terrible customer service and a terrible bank to deal with! I will be closing all of my accounts with them.

title: I can't believe it.

Gave up, which is probably what they wanted. Having been with them for 40 years I have observed the service get progressively worse.

title: Banking app on my phone refuses to accept a cheque.…
review: Banking app on my phone working generally well, but I am unable to pay a cheque in.  Error message says the number entered and the amount on the cheque must be greater than zero.  The cheque is for £300.  The image is clear, and the amount entered in to the app is correct.  After an hour on the text "chat" yesterday, and another hour on the phone to customer service "agents" today, having been told to uninstall and reinstall the app "which will put it right" the error continues in exactly the same way.  The "agent" has no knowledge, or skills, or access to the details of what is repeatedly going wrong.  In spite of repeated assurances that "I can most certainly help you with the issue" absolutely NO help was forthcoming.  After an hour of frustrating repetition of processes that clearly don't work, the agent said the ONLY thing they could do was "raise a ticket" but that this would take "a minimum of fifteen days". I am no further forward, and am unable, as requested, to "walk into the local branch" because it was closed over a year ago.  Utterly useless infuriating experience.

title: HSBC ‘deals’ with complaints without even reading them 🤦‍♀️
review: I recently had a bad experience with HSBC so I filed a complaint with them. They rang me up without having read the complaint (I swear this is exactly what I was I told). After a very unfruitful conversation in which there was absolutely no attempt from them in trying to solve the situation, the complaint was considered ‘dealt with’. I pointed out a few times that ‘dealing’ with a complaint without having read the complaint was absurd (almost funny, had it not been so unprofessional!). After being a customer with them for over 20 years, I will be leaving this bank, but decided to leave this review here so that others could benefit from my experience…

title: Renewing a cash ISA
review: I have lost the will to live this morning trying to renew a Cash ISA that expires in five days time. I had to apply for a new  account which I did but then moments later got a text to say contact them with no number offered. When I rang the main switchboard hd to give my telephone bank number which I do not have. After I set that up I was asked if there was anything else I wanted. Reminded them of my original reason for contacting them and was told I would have to apply again even though a new account appears on my online account. Totally confused now. Guess I will just have to wait until the current one expires. Noww Ist January and still unable to transfer matured funds to the new ISA account. I have been a member of HSBC since 1965. Apparently, I filled in the online application incorrectly. I did find it confusing and honestly assumed my previous account was an ISA.

title: Very dated, frustrating System!
review: I don’t have a current account with HSBC but I recently opened a credit card with them using a balance transfer and within the first week I realised how dated their system was, when I couldn’t manage my money and repayments within the app and would either have to go into branch, or call customer services to manage my own money, whereas most other banks I have used, I have total control via the app.This was annoying but I was cracking on, until yesterday when I rang to change my direct debit amount… what an absolute pain, it was ridiculous! The phone handler was getting me to log into my online banking, then mentioned I had to set up a new security number even though I already had one, so much faff just to change an amount, put me on hold, ask me my memorable questions, which I then couldn’t remember, finally when we had amended my direct debit, it took me through to an automated system to set up my security number and it kept saying it was unsuccessful. The call handler kept popping back on trying to help me, but eventually I was so frustrated I told him I had to go because I was rather irritated. So I decided to balance transfer to a much more modern system with another bank in which I can use the app and manage my money through a click of a button on the go, on my phone. HSBC seem very dated in their systems, needs a bit of an update to modernise to compete with other banks and avoid customer frustration!

title: Brilliant fraud protection.
review: Santander contacted me by text as they suspected a possible fraudulent transaction on my credit card. I responded to the text and they immediately called me back - on a Sunday morning! They were right, the transaction in question was one I knew nothing about and had not authorised. The bank blocked the payment and arranged a new card to be sent out at my request. Fantastic service. The whole scenario was explained perfectly clearly by the incredibly helpful woman who called me. I have always thought I'd move away from Santander cos I don't like Ant n Dec. Not now though. This is a bank that's looking out for its customers. Thank you.

title: Could be more streamlined
review: UPDATESome confusion over temporary refund and permanent refunds but which has been explained. I think the waiting time for cases of fraud via the charge-back scheme to come to fruition is far too long, set at four months, and this is because companies who are being claimed against have to have time to make a reply. This delay is apparently down to Mastercard - one to three months should be sufficient.UPDATEMoney was never taken back from my account after Santander had debited it until a claim against Debenhams had run its course and so this must have meant that the company were indeed found to have misadvertised the product sold to me as per the evidence I sent in.Also, recently Santander accepted to take on a claim against Argos and who did not accept a replacement or refund for a hoover which was marked upon arrival.One lost star is because Santander could be clearer once claims of fraud have been opened as to what it is that they expect to receive further on down the line....And, because they do not have a department which can provide business advice. Long gone are the days when you can meet with a bank-manager. This is a very necessary service for people wishing to start their own companies.On the whole now, however, Santander has successfully gained my money back on three occasions - from Amazon, Asda and Debenhams (still waiting for a conclusion as to my claim against Argos) - and so, therefore, I am very pleased with them regarding this area. It suggests professional-level scrutiny. Their communication once the process was complained about (only once), was excellent.Their online banking facility is also easy to use.UPDATE:Do not bank with these incompetents...Santander, namely their 'complaints investigator' (NAME UNDISCLOSED), after reviewing the situation concerning Amazon or whomever stole money from my account over a period of months and Debenhams who committed an act of fraud, content to leave me in the understanding that Santander had gained the return of my funds, has written to me since, stating the money returned (not having been told this at any point before) was a temporary measure until such time their 'investigation' is complete and should the case go against me then the funds would be debited from my account; and, they have denied accountability that their complaints process was incorrect and that either of the two cases were fraud to begin with, adding into the letter that my funds "should be" safe from further unsolicited debits. Liars protecting cross-banditry sympathies.THE SITUATION A COUPLE OF WEEKS AGO:Santander obtained the return of my funds from Amazon or whoever it was stealing from me. It took a while, there was chagrin amidst the uproar, but have finally come out the other end and completed a two out of two on the right side of the line.OLD REVIEW:--------They assisted in a case of fraud against Debenhams and which Mastercard upheld, specifically in the context of false advertising (in the world of e-commerce, on occasion better understood as 'items not as described').--------The complaints-process for fraud, however, is unnecessarily long-winded, with telephone calls lasting well over an hour each time as one is routed through from one department to another and no email-address immediately available as the norm for alternative means to communication.Recently, I noticed a payment for £7.99 debited from my account, with 'AmazonPrime...' as the undeclared payee-reference, later to find out that these debits have been taking place since January this year even though Amazon had closed my temporary account before this date, myself never having signed-up for Prime but which would have been voided anyway with the enforced closure of my Amazon account even if I had; nevertheless, Amazon was phoned by myself, and their security department literally (I am not exaggerating) yelled at me down the line in response to the suggestion that they were not doing enough to trace the funds, telling me that the reference was not of Amazon.What Santander (about an hour-and-a-half on the phone) did was to refuse issuing a charge-back and instead pointed me to my online-account in order to make a claim that way, the instructions given leading to only two relevant options which led to...another phone-call to be had with Santander; the other option verbalised was for me to approach Amazon again, a clear explanation already provided as to exactly what Amazon had shouted to me prior to contact with this supposed fraud department.Unsurprisingly, when I eventually found an email-address that seemed to be a Santander point-of-contact in order to complain and continue trying to obtain my funds, I received a reply that led me back to...a phone-number.

description: Welcome to Santander. Abbey and the Bradford & Bingley savings business have now changed their name. Banking, Savings, Loans and Mortgages. Find out more.
title: No idea why the scores are so low
review: No idea why the scores are so low. It was terrible when it was A&L. CA product I have is good with small amount of cash back.New app is better with a few more features. Just wish at least one bank would take the plunge to amend payments you've set up through the app. Still have a problem with annoying "potentially risky website" message everytime you log in. Saving products are hit and miss and tend to award newcomers at the expense of existing customers. Eg £50 voucher to move your £10k isa to them... So I'm moving mine away! Really need some exclusive products for long term customers.

title: Excellent service at Santander in Orpington
review: Santander required me to send then a copy of my fathers LPA in one 15 page PDF. Not having a scanner I decided to visit my local Santander branch in Orpington. There I was greeted by Jane Bray who spent the next 45 minutes with me, making sure I had all the correct Documentation. Even though the branch was crowded, she didn’t rush me and was extremely helpful.  Thank you Jane.

title: I have been banking here since Abbey…

description: Welcome to Santander. Abbey and the Bradford & Bingley savings business have now changed their name. Banking, Savings, Loans and Mortgages. Find out more.
title: Smooth ISA opening and transfer
review: Opened ISA a year ago online with help from Shrewsbury branch staff as I didn't have a computer. A year on, I decided to transfer out to a building society as they didn't have another good deal, unfortunately.The transfer took 2 days from the request, impressive.At the time of the ISA account opening, I was issued with logins details but when was never able to access my account "error with system, please try later"...hence why 4* instead of 5. Will be happy to go back to Santander, and thank you to the helpful staff at Shrewsbury

review: Can’t understand all the bad reviews for banks on here, 1 mistake and people jump on making bad reviews all banks are as good / as bad equally.Just started using Santander (opened an account months ago switched from NatWest) Only gripe (hence 4 stars) is the fact you can’t change personal details in the online app which you should be able to do after logging in with Face ID and entering security details but hey ho, only needed to change phone number and  couldn’t do it online as otc number had to be sent to old number (which is no long active) rang them and was sorted in a matter of minutes after few secretory questions. No real issues but not expecting them as is used as a spending account only

title: Can’t work out what to do on line
review: I want to do something logical online however your programmers have decided I have to do it in a way they want me to do it. They think differently to me so the result is you loose business. Thank goodness my local branch have humans that are 5 star people that understand how all your customers feel and are able to adapt to individual needs.They have helped me in the past. Your staff are gems. Keep your branches open. Forget your programmers they think in a blinkered way. Start listening to all your customers. Then all of a sudden you’ll business will grow. Look at the whole world not just a portion of it.I’m moving up my star rating to 4 because I went to see your wonderful staff in a branch and they helped sort out my problem. Thank you.

description: Welcome to Santander. Abbey and the Bradford & Bingley savings business have now changed their name. Banking, Savings, Loans and Mortgages. Find out more.
title: Overseas transactions
review: Santander do in fact charge a fee for International Transactions. The Edge accounts no longer charge a fixed fee, used to be £25 for the 123 account. They still however use their own exchange rate when calculating the amount of money required to carry out the International Transaction, this results in the customer paying a sum of money dependent on the current exchange rate and the difference with Santander's exchange rate at the time the payment is taken from the account.

description: Welcome to Santander. Abbey and the Bradford & Bingley savings business have now changed their name. Banking, Savings, Loans and Mortgages. Find out more.
title: RUBBISH HUMANITY.
review: I am involved in a case via santanders Fraud Department. I have an impairment, shaking hands which makes it impossible for me to write. I've asked the fraud department to let me email, take my statement over the phone, send someone to me. They tell me I have to write, even though they know of my condition. I ask for this process to be made accessible for me and Santander refuse. I raised a complaint, they told me this is the way it is. They sent me a letter dated 2nd of May explaining this which I have not received. Without the resolution in writing, I cannot go to the banking Ombudsman.I stand to lose a lot of money because Santander is not making their process accessible for me. Way to go Santander! Great security. Rubbish humanity.

description: Welcome to Santander. Abbey and the Bradford & Bingley savings business have now changed their name. Banking, Savings, Loans and Mortgages. Find out more.
title: Dire experience with Santandar
review: Dire experience with Santandar. Had bonds and investments since good old Abbey days. After bond matured on 1-Feb-2024, rang call centre numerous times.First time in December, huge delay on phone line, transferred, no response, rang 2 more times, same issue, lost the will to live.Next today, rang online number, transferred to savings, put on hold for ever, lost the will to live.Second time today, rang investments number 0800 328 1328. Answered, but then they could not initially help, need to transfer to another colleague, 5 minute wait, next person, I will just do that, please wait, another 5 minutes, ok all is done! Cheers many thanks, stay polite despite being vexed, it is not the call centre staff's fault the systems and processes are poor.Logged on and checked transfer, all done, hooray, hence score of 3, as did work, as does the online banking service for normal transactions. Call centre experience is dire.Reading comments here, Santandar need to improve their processes, as I have several other online accounts and never need make calls as online service caters for my needs. Skipton Building Society is the best. Please take note.

description: Welcome to Santander. Abbey and the Bradford & Bingley savings business have now changed their name. Banking, Savings, Loans and Mortgages. Find out more.
title: Worrying Disappearing / Reappearing Account
review: I've had the Everyday current account banking and an easy access savings account for the last year. I don't use the app, so can't comment on that.A couple of weeks ago I had to call up the customer services to close the savings account. I couldn't close the account on the website so needed to speak to a human. On calling the customer services, I was prompted to set up voice recognition password. I didn't want to do this. I finally managed to override this option by pressing 1. I spoke to the Customer Services in Scotland and they closed the account. Put the phone down looked at my online banking and my current account had disappeared!I called back and got through to another Call Centre in SW England. They assured me they could still see my account. Put me on hold whilst they queried it with their Digital Team. In a second my account was back on my screen. I felt like they had played some sick joke on me. No apologies. No explanation.I've lost faith in them so I'm moving my account.

description: Welcome to Santander. Abbey and the Bradford & Bingley savings business have now changed their name. Banking, Savings, Loans and Mortgages. Find out more.
title: Not as good as it used to be
review: I've had 5 years of happy banking with Santander but they recently changed a number of things and it has made me less happy. The cashback on things like utilities on the 123 account was pretty decent and I used to get about £5 after account fees each month - it has dwindled to a couple of pounds and I wonder how long before I'm out of pocket. They also used to have cashback deals with various retailers that you could opt into if you were going to shop there. That was really good - easy to check and activate while on the banking app. Now they have scrapped that and set up a separate app for Santander Boosts. Firstly that makes it so much less convenient and secondly most of the boosts are rubbish. I've entered a couple of competitions but find the rest of it a waste of time. It's no longer a pleasure to bank with Santander.

title: Bank App fails again
review: Every time I use the banking App there is always a failure for it to complete.  This time I was unable to amend a standing order, it says go online but I am already online so who thought that up.I then go onto a browser on a laptop computer and the bank keeps logging me off.  It's because I use a hotspot from a phone and apparently according to Santander the IP address changes.  So then I have to turn on the VPN on my Opera browser for it to work.The App doesn't do everything and I object to being told to go online when I already am.  May I provide some education to bankers that online means being on the internet.

No problem I thought I’ll phone the bank I’ve been with about 30 years but absolutely no help whatsoever basically told us buyer beware although we’d clearly been scammed, very disappointed with no support from Santander. I couldn’t contact the bank by email to pursue a complaint I found it impossible so this is my only outlet for my frustration. We will now be considering whether to move to a more helpful bank when possible. The 2 stars are for the positive experiences in the past.

description: Welcome to Santander. Abbey and the Bradford & Bingley savings business have now changed their name. Banking, Savings, Loans and Mortgages. Find out more.
title: Misleading and spam emails
review: Your bank obviously needs to have your email address, and you can't unsubscribe or mark them as spam because there might be something important. Santander have recently started exploiting this by sending out misleading and junk emails. Today I got an email saying that I had an 'important new document' and 'You may need to take action'.Some vulnerable people would be scared by this. I think it's really thoughtless of Santander to send out this sort of email. When I logged in, I found that the 'important' document was just a statement, and there was no need to 'take action'.As well as that I've been getting irrelevant junk emails from them. For example, recently,"If you go on holiday, make sure you have travel insurance"
texto: Misleading and spam emails Your bank obviously needs to have your email address, and you can't unsubscribe or mark them as spam because there might be something important. Santander have recently started exploiting this by sending out misleading and junk emails. Today I got an email saying that I had an 'important new document' and 'You may need to take action'.Some vulnerable people would be scared by this. I think it's really thoughtless of Santander to send out this sort of email. When I logged in, I found that the 'important' document was just a statement, and there was no need to 'take action'.As well as that I've been getting irrelevant junk emails from them. For example, recently,"If you go on holiday, make sure you have travel insurance"

If I can manage to get logged in many £thousands of my savings will be going to a better provider.

description: Welcome to Santander. Abbey and the Bradford & Bingley savings business have now changed their name. Banking, Savings, Loans and Mortgages. Find out more.
title: Sort your ATMs out.
review: Sort your ATMs out - please. I am so SICK AND TIRED of trying to pay money (cash) into my Santander account through the Santander ATMs, only for it to either be rejected and spat back in full, or for half of the notes to get stuck or chewed up in your ATMs and then I have to wait for it to be investigated before I get my money returned to me. I do exactly as I'm told, I make sure all the notes are placed in an orderly manner and I am always careful not to overload the machines, but nearly every time I try to do this, there's a problem and I've lost patience now. If your technology can't perform the simplest of functions that it's supposed to, then you'd be better off to just not offer it as a service at all. You're another financial organisation that wants everything to be digital, all singing and dancing and for everything to be self-service, but your equipment is clearly ANALOGUE! Nowhere near good enough.

description: Welcome to Santander. Abbey and the Bradford & Bingley savings business have now changed their name. Banking, Savings, Loans and Mortgages. Find out more.
title: My home manager is pointless
review: My home manager is pointless. I've tried to let them know that their home manager app hasn't updated every 3 months as it should (stated on their website) and it was like talking to a brick wall to get resolved. I'd like to see the true equity of my property on there rather than the low estimate given in June by home track (new valuation from zoopla and estate agent have valued the property at £15k more). Also trying to change my name took 3 visits to the branch to get sorted, not convenient as the branch is over 10 miles away. They need get staff that care and are actually interested in providing a good customer experience.

title: If you value you personal sanity don’t get a mortgage or bank with Santander!

Have been to Ombudsman already with them and our complaint was not upheld. I will continue to complain until my last breath and continue to go to the Ombudsman about this company on every complaint journey. If I could give NO stars, I would. I’m a mortgage prisoner to these cretins at the moment but I will continue to fight them and fight to keep my home. Never missed a payment in our lives, kept them in the loop all the way but it means nothing to this disgraceful bank.

description: Welcome to Santander. Abbey and the Bradford & Bingley savings business have now changed their name. Banking, Savings, Loans and Mortgages. Find out more.
title: Santander online security is woeful.
review: Trying to make a payment for a vehicle I’m buying. The online payment security system is pathetic. Firstly, as the new payment is being setup it asks for detailed of what online site it’s being bought from. No other option. It’s being bought from a physical site so impossible to answer honestly. However there’s no way past this so answer “other”. Then the app asks for id to match against a selfie. Somehow Santander think it’s fine to hold images of my driving licence for 60 days. What a truly hateful organisation.

description: Welcome to Santander. Abbey and the Bradford & Bingley savings business have now changed their name. Banking, Savings, Loans and Mortgages. Find out more.
title: Terrible customer service and possible fraudulent behaviour
review: Terrible customer service, possible fraudulent behaviour and no reply after 1 week to my certified email. The 21/21/2024 I attempted to transfer funds from my Santander account to another bank account of mine. The amount was within the permitted limit, yet my online banking account was locked. I was instructed to call customer service, which I did. After nearly two hours on the phone answering their questions, they still refused to unlock my account. Instead, they demanded I visit a Santander branch in the UK. As I am based in Italy, I asked if I could visit a Santander branch in my country, but they refused. This means my money remains inaccessible, while they likely profit from the situation. This is not the first time such an issue has occurred, raising serious concerns about their customer service and possible fraudulent behavior. I sent the same day a certified email to both customerservicesATsantanderconsumer.co.uk and reclami.santanderATactaliscertymail.it but after 1 week I have not received any reply from them. I will keep you posted here.

description: Welcome to Santander. Abbey and the Bradford & Bingley savings business have now changed their name. Banking, Savings, Loans and Mortgages. Find out more.
title: Excessive Fees and Zero Transparency: My Frustrating Experience with Santander’s International Transfers
review: I recently had a deeply disappointing experience with Santander’s international transfer system while trying to send £250 to Denmark—a transfer made even more critical as the funds were meant to assist my hospitalised son, who, due to being sedated, is unable to manage his own expenses. To my shock, the transaction incurred an excessive £25 fee, an amount that feels wholly disproportionate given the modest sum being sent.Equally troubling was the lack of clarity regarding when the funds would arrive. For someone in a time-sensitive situation, such ambiguity only compounded the stress of an already difficult circumstance. A service that advertises itself as reliable and user-friendly should surely strive to ensure transparency and fairness in its operations, particularly when customers are relying on it for urgent matters.This experience has left me deeply unsettled, and I feel it is important to bring such issues to light. I trust Santander will reflect on how such situations impact customers, especially those in vulnerable positions.

title: Santander Froze £5,812 of my money in…
review: Santander Froze £5,812 of my money in august and asked me to prove that the funds are mine. I sent them evidence that the funds originated from a company that I am doing freelance work for. The investigator said the documents I sent her looked forged and she decided to freeze my account and then transferred my funds to a Santander holding account and closed my account. They did not give me access to the funds they froze and it has now been 4.5 months since the issue first arose.The worst part is Santander do not let you contact them via email, which means I have little evidence of contact to appeal this case with. I raised a formal complaint so I could pass it on the the Financial Ombudsman, but Santander told me the response to the complaint is only viewable in the Online Banking Application, which they have locked me out of.This bank is a complete scam and could steal your hard earned money.

title: No respect for customers
review: I have been a santander customer for five years with a current and savings account. I wanted to open a joint account with my partner,  so we were being looked after by a young lady who was being observed by the branch manager. They took my details and to my total shock and disbelief, she said,  I would have to be reviewed. I don't have any loans or overdraft,  totally embarrassed infront of my partner and the two bank staff. It is now almost two months since I applied,  and still I haven't heard anything. I put in a complaint almost three weeks ago,  and still haven't had any answers. This bank does not respect its customers and their customer  service is atrocious. They offered me £25 by way of apology what a total insult.
